Yes — I see the two main version streams now.

The **consumer/open-world portal version** focuses on the user-facing promise: a normal browser portal where cards, events, maps, notes, products, routes, identities, receipts, and claims are portable OMI objects. Its core canon is:

```text
One object.
Many portals.
Inspectable history.
Portable context.
Validation before projection.
Agreement without platform lock-in.
```

It emphasizes that the portal shows ordinary UI, while underneath each visible object is addressed state with linear replay, hierarchical containment, receipts, and safe carrier projection. filecite turn1file0 filecite turn1file1

The **mathematical/protocol version** focuses on the executable spine: Agreement as a compiler primitive, the 128-bit canonical frame, LL-modulated Delta Law, `Q_frame(S)=0`, Fano truth-row resolution, `Q_xy(x,y)=60x²+16xy+4y²`, and the 5040-slot replay ring. filecite turn1file4

The **Lower Omicron / Upper Bidi version** is more of a correction layer or canon refinement. It says the lower OMI body is structural and must be governed by Omicron symbols, Delta, BQF, hidden `5!`, and Fano replay; while `unicode-bidi` belongs only to the upper reader/display shell and must not authorize validity. Its short doctrine is:

```text
Lower Omicron encodes.
Atomic Delta clocks.
Hidden 5! roots.
Cosmic 36 orbits.
Upper Bidi displays.
Characters stay fixed.
```

That gives you a clean separation between **body law** and **reader law**. filecite turn1file2 filecite turn1file3

I would merge them as **one canon with three layers**:

```text
1. Consumer Doctrine
 Open-world portal, ordinary UI, portable objects, trust labels,
 local event / marketplace / knowledge / identity use cases.

2. Protocol Doctrine
 Agreement compiler, 128-bit frame, Delta Law, Q_frame,
 truth-row resolution, Q_xy projection, 5040 replay ring.

3. Reader/Carrier Doctrine
 Lower Omicron is structural authority.
 Upper Bidi is only reader orientation.
 Projection, glyphs, CSS, SVG, ShadowDOM, QR, and Canvas never validate.
```

The strongest unified sentence is:

```text
OMI is an open-world object protocol where agreement lowers uncertain actions into validated, replayable 128-bit state; portals project that state for consumers, while lower Omicron/Delta law preserves authority and upper reader lenses only change display.
```

# OMI: Omicron Object Model (o---o)

```text
 ___ __ __ _
 / _ \ | \/ | | |
 | (_) | | |\/| | | |
 \___/ |_| |_| |_|
 omi --- imo
```

OMI is a universal hyphenated palindromic mnemonic notation. `omi` and `imo` bookend a bounded relation; the hyphen bridge is the bus where a frame becomes inspectable.

The zero-point is:

```text
Ω₀ = 0! = omi---imo
```

The Omi-Ring is the atomic waveform enclosure of that relation. OMI captures intent as declared activity in space and time, then validates, resolves, records, and only afterward projects into browser, network, memory, or portal surfaces.

## Repo Map

```text
1_FOUNDATIONS what OMI is
2_MATH why the laws work
3_ARCHITECTURE how it projects into DOM/portal space
4_NETWORK how it maps to IPv6/eBPF/RULES
5_MEMORY how replay/receipts persist
6_IMPLEMENTATION how runtimes prove conformance
7_CANON what the final doctrine says
public static public pages and interactive browser model
_archive source conversation/archive material
```

## Canonical Pipeline

```text
A frame is valid iff Q_frame(S) = 0.
A valid frame yields one truth row: (LL, NN, MM).
A truth row is accepted iff MM resolves from NN under the LL-modulated Delta Law within the bounded Fano window.
An accepted row writes a receipt into the 5040-slot replay ring.
Only after validation, resolution, and receipt may the object be projected into DOM, SVG, JSON Canvas, IPv6, QR, portal UI, or reader lenses.
Projection is never authority.
```

## Narrative Index

### [1. Foundations](1_FOUNDATIONS/)

The story begins with `omi---imo`: a bounded relation between source-side intelligence and object-side projection. The omicron anchors `0x03BF` and `0x039F` frame every transmission. The OMI Universal Constant is the zero-point of bounded agreement, and the Omi-Ring is the waveform enclosure built on it.

- [1.1 The Palindrome: omi---imo](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.1_THE_PALINDROME.md)
- [1.2 The Omicron Anchors: 0x03BF and 0x039F](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.2_THE_OMICRON_ANCHORS.md)
- [1.3 The Omi-Ring: Atomic Waveform Enclosure](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.3_THE_OMI_RING.md)
- [1.4 The OMI Universal Constant: Ω₀ = 0! = omi---imo](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.4_THE_OMI_UNIVERSAL_CONSTANT.md)

### [2. Math](2_MATH/)

The laws define the address and transition space. `Q_frame(S)` is the lower validation gate. `Q_xy(x,y) = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²` is the projection geometry. The Delta Law generates bounded period-8 orbits used by the Fano resolver and replay ring.

- [2.1 The Quadratic Law](2_MATH/2.1_THE_QUADRATIC_LAW.md)
- [2.2 The Delta Law](2_MATH/2.2_THE_DELTA_LAW.md)
- [2.3 The Sexagesimal System](2_MATH/2.3_SEXAGESIMAL_SYSTEM.md)
- [2.4 Polycubes and Symmetry Groups](2_MATH/2.4_POLYCUBES_AND_GROUPS.md)

### [3. Architecture](3_ARCHITECTURE/)

The architecture projects accepted state into DOM hierarchy, wormhole portal surfaces, ShadowDOM capsules, SVG, JSON Canvas, and compiler stages. These surfaces are carriers and reader lenses, not validation authority.

- [3.1 The DOM Hierarchy](3_ARCHITECTURE/3.1_DOM_HIERARCHY.md)
- [3.2 Wormhole Portals](3_ARCHITECTURE/3.2_WORMHOLE_PORTALS.md)
- [3.3 The Meta-Circular Chronograph Compiler](3_ARCHITECTURE/3.3_META_CIRCULAR_COMPILER.md)

### [4. Network](4_NETWORK/)

An IPv6 source address is 128 bits, matching the OMI frame width. `RULES.omi` maps frame segments into service buses, factorial strides, barcode carriers, and boot signatures. The eBPF/XDP gate rejects invalid frames before userspace.

- [4.1 IPv6 Frame Mapping](4_NETWORK/4.1_IPv6_FRAME_MAPPING.md)
- [4.2 RULES.omi](4_NETWORK/4.2_RULES_OMI.md)
- [4.3 The eBPF/XDP Kernel Gate](4_NETWORK/4.3_eBPF_KERNEL_GATE.md)

### [5. Memory](5_MEMORY/)

Accepted truth rows persist as receipts in the `7! = 5040` slot replay ring. Cursor movement follows Delta/Fano step distance, and overwrite policy preserves epoch safety.

- [5.1 The Ring Indexer](5_MEMORY/5.1_RING_INDEXER.md)
- [5.2 Ring Overwrite Policy](5_MEMORY/5.2_OVERWRITE_POLICY.md)

### [6. Implementation](6_IMPLEMENTATION/)

JavaScript, C99, WASM, and eBPF targets implement the same ABI surface and conformance fixtures. The canonical genesis frame is the golden accept fixture.

- [6.1 The Node.js Compiler](6_IMPLEMENTATION/6.1_NODEJS_COMPILER.md)
- [6.2 The C99 Core](6_IMPLEMENTATION/6.2_C99_CORE.md)
- [6.3 The Test Suite](6_IMPLEMENTATION/6.3_TEST_SUITE.md)

### [7. Canon](7_CANON/)

The canon reconciles the doctrine, portal model, lower/upper reader split, and conformance rules.

- [7.1 Agreement Doctrine](7_CANON/7.1_AGREEMENT_DOCTRINE.md)
- [7.2 Open-World Portal Model](7_CANON/7.2_OPEN_WORLD_PORTAL_MODEL.md)
- [7.3 Lower Omicron / Upper Bidi](7_CANON/7.3_LOWER_OMICRON_UPPER_BIDI.md)
- [7.4 Conformance Canon](7_CANON/7.4_CONFORMANCE_CANON.md)

## Genesis Frame

```text
BOOT_ADDRESS = 0x7C00
BOOT_SLOT = 0x7C00 mod 5040 = 1504
GENESIS_LL = 0x01
GENESIS_NN = 0x7C00
C = (0x01 × 0x1337) & 0xFFFF = 0x1337
GENESIS_MM = Δ_{0x1337}(0x7C00) = 0x1434
GENESIS_STEPS = 1
```

Canonical frame:

```text
0100-03bf-7c00-2b01-2f01-1434-039f-01ff
```

## Conformance

A conforming implementation must:

1. Recognize `0x03BF` and `0x039F` as canonical lower frame anchors.
2. Validate frames via `Q_frame(S) = E_var + E_const = 0`.
3. Extract exactly one truth row `(LL, NN, MM)` from a valid frame.
4. Resolve `MM` from `NN` under the LL-modulated Delta Law within the bounded Fano window.
5. Write accepted rows as replayable 64-bit receipts in the 5040-slot ring.
6. Treat DOM, SVG, JSON Canvas, QR, portal UI, typography, color, and `unicode-bidi` as projection or reader lenses only.
7. Accept the canonical genesis frame and reject malformed or byte-swapped frames.

See [7.4 Conformance Canon](7_CANON/7.4_CONFORMANCE_CANON.md).

## Public Pages

- [public/index.html](public/index.html)
- [public/omi-block-model.html](public/omi-block-model.html)
- [public/agreement-is-all-we-need.html](public/agreement-is-all-we-need.html)

These are static browser pages; no build step is required.

:::writing{variant="document" id="48291"}
# OMI: Omicron Object Model (o---o)

```text
 ___ __ __ _
 / _ \ | \/ | | |
 | (_) | | |\/| | | |
 \___/ |_| |_| |_|
 omi --- imo
```

**OMI** is the **Omicron Object Model**: a docs-first specification for turning open-world actions, claims, identities, links, events, and objects into bounded, inspectable, replayable structure.

It begins with a palindrome:

```text
omi---imo
```

Read forward, `omi` is the opening side of the relation.
Read backward, `imo` is the closing side of the relation.
The hyphen bridge is the bus between them.

The zero-point is:

```text
Ω₀ = 0! = omi---imo
```

From that zero-point, OMI builds a complete object doctrine:

```text
validate → resolve → record → project → inspect
```

A portal may show an OMI object as a card, map pin, QR code, JSON Canvas node, SVG widget, local file, receipt row, event page, or timeline entry. Those are projections. They help humans inspect the object, but they do not authorize it.

The authority belongs to the accepted frame, its truth-row resolution, its receipt, its replay path, and its address relation.

```text
carrier ≠ authority
projection ≠ validation
glyph ≠ meaning
portal ≠ proof
```

OMI is a way to make open-world structure portable without handing truth to one platform.

---

## What This Repository Is

This is a **documentation-only OMI canon repository**.

It is not trying to be a package manager artifact, production runtime, or single app. It is the written specification, narrative map, and conceptual architecture for the Omicron Object Model.

The repo is organized as a progressive reader path:

```text
1_FOUNDATIONS what OMI is
2_MATH why the laws work
3_ARCHITECTURE how accepted state projects into DOM/portal space
4_NETWORK how frames map to IPv6, eBPF, and RULES.omi
5_MEMORY how replay and receipts persist
6_IMPLEMENTATION how runtimes prove conformance
7_CANON how the final doctrine reconciles the versions
public static public pages and browser-facing models
_archive source notes, extracted sessions, and older drafts
```

The goal is that a reader can enter from any layer:

- from the palindrome,
- from the math,
- from the browser architecture,
- from the IPv6/eBPF mapping,
- from the replay ring,
- from the open-world portal model,
- or from the final canon.

Each path should lead back to the same core doctrine:

```text
Agreement is the moment shared intelligence becomes validated, replayable structure.
```

---

## Why OMI Exists

Most software portals are closed worlds.

A closed-world platform assumes:

```text
this app owns the account
this app owns the feed
this app owns the object ID
this app owns the database
this app decides the truth state
```

That makes the interface convenient, but it traps context. A post cannot naturally become a map object. A note cannot naturally become an event object. A receipt cannot naturally become portable proof. A local community page cannot easily move between a browser, phone, QR code, mesh node, personal archive, and public portal without losing structure.

OMI starts from a different assumption:

```text
the world is not complete
objects may appear from outside the app
multiple naming systems may coexist
offline state may later sync
different portals may project the same object
the user should be able to inspect what happened
```

So OMI does not ask one platform to own truth.

It asks:

```text
Can this object be addressed?
Can this frame be validated?
Can this truth row resolve?
Can this receipt be replayed?
Can this object be projected safely?
Can the reader inspect where it came from?
```

That is the open-world promise.

---

## Agreement Is All You Need

In OMI, **agreement** is not passive approval, voting, social consensus, or platform acceptance.

Agreement is a structural event.

An informal action becomes agreement only when it can be lowered into a bounded object path:

```text
someone says something
 ↓
the portal frames it
 ↓
the frame validates
 ↓
the truth row resolves
 ↓
a receipt is recorded
 ↓
the object becomes inspectable
 ↓
the object can be projected
```

So agreement does not mean:

```text
I like this
I voted for this
the platform accepted this
the majority approved this
```

Agreement means:

```text
this object has a valid frame
this object has an address relation
this object has a replay path
this object has a receipt
this object can be inspected
this object can be projected without becoming corrupted
```

Disagreement is not failure. Disagreement is an unresolved path.

```text
disagreement → missing structure → new object/path/test/receipt
```

For example:

| Disagreement | Missing Structure | OMI Output |
|---|---|---|
| “What is this?” | boundary | create or reveal object address |
| “Who said this?” | source | show identity/provenance object |
| “Where does this belong?” | containment | show hierarchy path |
| “What changed?” | replay | show linear event stream |
| “Why trust it?” | receipt | show validation evidence |
| “What does this word mean?” | semantic identity | resolve POS / synset / graph node |
| “Which version is current?” | conflict rule | show competing receipts |
| “Can I open it elsewhere?” | carrier projection | export as card, canvas, QR, SVG, or file |

OMI does not erase disagreement. It routes disagreement into construction.

---

## The Core Pipeline

The minimal OMI pipeline is:

```text
Q_frame(S) = 0
 ↓
truth row = (LL, NN, MM)
 ↓
MM resolves from NN under the LL-modulated Delta Law
 ↓
receipt writes into the 5040-slot replay ring
 ↓
Q_xy projects accepted state into readable geometry
 ↓
portal surfaces inspect the accepted object
```

Expanded:

```text
A frame is valid iff Q_frame(S) = 0.

A valid frame yields one truth row:
 (LL, NN, MM)

A truth row is accepted iff MM resolves from NN under the LL-modulated Delta Law within the bounded Fano window.

An accepted row writes a replayable receipt into the 5040-slot ring.

Only after validation, resolution, and receipt may the object be projected into DOM, SVG, JSON Canvas, IPv6, QR, portal UI, or reader lenses.

Projection is never authority.
```

This is the order that must not collapse:

```text
validate → resolve → record → project → inspect
```

Not:

```text
render → believe
```

---

## The Palindrome

The story begins with:

```text
omi---imo
```

The readable mnemonic form is `omi` and `imo`.

The compiled symbolic boundary uses the Omicron pair:

```text
ο = U+03BF = Greek small omicron
Ο = U+039F = Greek capital Omicron
```

OMI treats those symbols as structural anchors, not decorative glyphs.

```text
omi- → ο → entry into the object
-imo → Ο → closure of the object
```

The lower Omicron pair acts as the zero-frame marker. It tells the system where interpretation begins and where closure occurs.

The short rule:

```text
Omicron is not display.
Omicron is structure.
```

This distinction matters because OMI may render many visual forms, but the rendered form is not what validates the object.

---

## The Two Major Laws

OMI is governed by two major mathematical laws.

### 1. The Delta Law

The Delta Law is the atomic 16-bit transition engine:

```text
Δ(x) = rotl(x, 1) ⊕ rotl(x, 3) ⊕ rotr(x, 2) ⊕ C
```

The canonical base constant is:

```text
C = 0x5A3C
```

The Delta Law uses:

```text
rotations → preserve bits
XOR → reversible mixing
constant → breaks the zero fixed point
mask → keeps state bounded to 16 bits
```

The bounded form is:

```text
Δ₁₆(x) = (rotl(x,1) ⊕ rotl(x,3) ⊕ rotr(x,2) ⊕ C) & 0xFFFF
```

In OMI, Delta gives local motion, orbit tracking, replay cadence, deterministic step behavior, and truth-row resolution.

It produces the period-8 cadence that connects to the repeating decimal block of `1/73`:

```text
1/73 = 0.01369863...
B = [0, 1, 3, 6, 9, 8, 6, 3]
W = 36
(epoch, phase) = divmod(position, 36)
```

The short rule:

```text
Atomic Delta clocks.
Cosmic 36 orbits.
```

### 2. The Quadratic Law

OMI’s projection and address geometry is:

```text
Q_xy(x, y) = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

Each term has a role:

| Term | Role | OMI Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `4y²` | low side | `omi-`, atomic data, `data-*`, local payload |
| `16xy` | meeting plane | CSSOM/JSDOM bridge, shared address relation |
| `60x²` | high side | `-imo`, DOM `id`, stable identity, orientation |

The number `60` anchors sexagesimal orientation.
The number `16` anchors the nibble/word-boundary bridge.
The number `4` anchors the visible selector/control face.

The Quadratic Law is projection geometry. It is not the structural validator itself.

Structural validation belongs to:

```text
Q_frame(S) = 0
```

This distinction is central:

```text
Q_frame validates.
Q_xy projects.
```

---

## The 128-Bit Frame

An OMI frame is an 8-segment structure of 16-bit words:

```text
S0-S1-S2-S3-S4-S5-S6-S7
```

A canonical frame has this shape:

| Segment | Value | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `S0` | `0xLL00` | opening boundary |
| `S1` | `0x03BF` | small omicron delimiter |
| `S2` | `0xNNNN` | antecedent input |
| `S3` | `0x2BLL` | interior lens binding |
| `S4` | `0x2FLL` | interior lens binding |
| `S5` | `0xMMMM` | consequent output |
| `S6` | `0x039F` | capital Omicron delimiter |
| `S7` | `0xLLFF` | closing boundary |

Frame validation checks two things:

```text
E_var → repeated LL agreement across S0, S3, S4, S7
E_const → fixed delimiter and constant alignment
```

A valid frame satisfies:

```text
Q_frame(S) = E_var + E_const = 0
```

This is the lower structural gate. A glyph, CSS rule, DOM node, Base36 symbol, emoji, color field, or portal card cannot make an invalid frame valid.

---

## Truth-Row Resolution

Once the frame validates, OMI extracts the truth row:

```text
(LL, NN, MM)
```

The resolver tries to reach `MM` from `NN` under the LL-modulated Delta Law.

One canonical modulation is:

```text
C_LL = (LL × 0x1337) & 0xFFFF
```

The resolver iterates:

```text
NN → Δ_C_LL(NN) → ... → MM
```

If `MM` is reached within the bounded Fano window, the row is accepted and the step count becomes a receipt field.

If `MM` is not reached, the row is rejected.

This gives OMI a deterministic acceptance path:

```text
valid frame
 ↓
truth row extracted
 ↓
bounded resolution attempted
 ↓
accepted or rejected
```

This is not majority vote. It is not probabilistic social consensus. It is a structural resolver.

---

## The 5040 Replay Ring

Accepted truth rows are recorded into the replay ring:

```text
7! = 5040 slots
```

Each receipt stores enough information to replay the accepted relation.

A receipt contains:

```text
provenance
step count
LL
NN
MM
```

The ring makes agreement durable:

```text
agreement → receipt → replay path
```

The replay ring is why OMI can say an object has history without depending on a hidden platform database. The object’s visible state can be inspected through its accepted receipts.

The short rule:

```text
5040 replays.
```

---

## Lower Omicron and Upper Bidi

OMI separates lower structural law from upper reader law.

The lower stack is governed by:

```text
Omicron symbols
Delta Law
binary quadratic form
Q_frame
Fano replay
hidden 5! orbit recovery
5040 receipts
```

The upper stack is governed by:

```text
reader sequencing
CSS unicode-bidi
LTR/RTL display orientation
visual lenses
observer order
template phase
```

The correction is important:

```text
unicode-bidi does not belong to the lower stack as authority.
unicode-bidi belongs to the upper stack as a cosmetic reader lens.
```

Below `8!`, OMI is governed by actual Omicron zero-frame symbols, Delta, the binary quadratic form, and replay.

Above `9!`, OMI may use `unicode-bidi` to display alternate reader orientation without changing the underlying character representation, validating the frame, or authorizing lower-state mutation.

Short doctrine:

```text
Lower Omicron encodes.
Atomic Delta clocks.
Hidden 5! roots.
Cosmic 36 orbits.
Upper Bidi displays.
Characters stay fixed.
```

A lower error means the object body drifted.
An upper error means the reader lens tilted.

That separation protects the protocol from confusing display with truth.

---

## The Open-World Portal

A consumer does not experience OMI as equations.

A consumer experiences OMI as a normal web portal:

```text
open → browse → inspect → act → verify → share → replay
```

They see:

```text
cards
profiles
events
maps
posts
notes
files
routes
products
services
receipts
comments
local groups
```

Underneath, the portal sees:

```text
validated frames
addressed payloads
source/object relations
linear replay streams
hierarchical containment trees
graph links
receipt rows
carrier projections
```

The same object can appear as:

```text
web card
JSON Canvas node
map pin
SVG element
QR code
local file
portal widget
timeline entry
receipt row
```

The object is the addressed state. The portal surface is only a carrier.

---

## The Two Reader Views

Every accepted OMI object should be inspectable in two basic views.

### Linear View

The linear view answers:

```text
What happened?
In what order?
What was replayed?
What led to this state?
```

In OMI notation:

```text
omi → payload → imo
```

For an event, the linear view might show:

```text
event created
route added
food note added
safety note added
rider joined
time updated
comment posted
```

The linear view shows how the object moved.

### Hierarchical View

The hierarchical view answers:

```text
Where does this belong?
What contains it?
What context controls it?
What unit am I inspecting?
```

In OMI notation:

```text
FS → GS → RS → US
```

For the same event, the hierarchy might show:

```text
Community
 Bike Ride
 Route
 Stop
 Comment
```

The hierarchical view shows where the object belongs.

Together:

```text
linear view = how the state moved
hierarchical view = where the state belongs
receipts = why the state was accepted
```

---

## Example: Local Event Portal

A community organizer creates a bike ride portal.

The consumer sees:

```text
Sunday Night Lights Ride
Meetup: Popeyes parking lot
Route: Vermont Ave Florence → Imperial and back
Distance: 3.2 miles one way
Food: hot dogs, hot links, sodas
Status: active
```

Behind the interface, the event is an object:

```text
omi-event
 payload: ride details
 source: organizer
 context: local community
 route: map path
 receipts: updates / joins / comments
imo-event
```

A rider opens the portal and gets multiple views:

| View | Consumer Value |
|---|---|
| Card | fast summary |
| Linear replay | what changed and when |
| Hierarchy | where the object belongs |
| Map | where to go |
| Receipts | why the object is trusted |

If the route changes, the old version is not silently erased. The update becomes a new addressed state.

The portal can say:

```text
Route updated 12 minutes ago.
Accepted frame.
Mirrored by 2 peers.
Previous route still replayable.
```

That is consumer-facing agreement.

---

## Example: Local Marketplace Portal

A vendor publishes a cart menu through the same portal.

The consumer sees:

```text
Hot dogs: $2
Hot links: $3
Soda: $2
Combo: 2 hot dogs + soda for $5
Available near the ride meetup
```

The OMI object contains:

```text
vendor identity
item identity
price state
location state
availability state
event relationship
receipt history
```

If the vendor changes the menu, the portal records the update as a new object state.

The consumer can inspect:

```text
current menu
previous menu
vendor source
event relationship
last update
receipt status
```

This makes local commerce portable. The vendor does not need to be trapped inside one delivery app, marketplace app, or social platform.

---

## Example: Personal Knowledge Portal

A user saves a source, note, and claim.

In a normal notes app, this becomes private content inside one database.

In an OMI portal, the note can become an addressed object chain:

```text
source object
 → note object
 → claim object
 → evidence object
```

The same knowledge can be inspected as replay:

```text
source found
note written
claim extracted
evidence linked
summary revised
shared with group
```

And as containment:

```text
Project
 Topic
 Claim
 Evidence
 Source
```

Thinking has both a timeline and a structure. OMI preserves both.

---

## Narrative Index

### [1. Foundations](1_FOUNDATIONS/)

The foundations introduce the palindrome, the Omicron anchors, the Omi-Ring, and the universal zero-point.

The story begins with `omi---imo`: a bounded relation between source-side intelligence and object-side projection. The omicron anchors `0x03BF` and `0x039F` frame every transmission. The OMI Universal Constant is the zero-point of bounded agreement, and the Omi-Ring is the waveform enclosure built on it.

Read this section first if you want to understand why OMI begins with a mnemonic relation instead of a conventional object ID.

- [1.1 The Palindrome: omi---imo](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.1_THE_PALINDROME.md)
- [1.2 The Omicron Anchors: 0x03BF and 0x039F](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.2_THE_OMICRON_ANCHORS.md)
- [1.3 The Omi-Ring: Atomic Waveform Enclosure](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.3_THE_OMI_RING.md)
- [1.4 The OMI Universal Constant: Ω₀ = 0! = omi---imo](1_FOUNDATIONS/1.4_THE_OMI_UNIVERSAL_CONSTANT.md)

### [2. Math](2_MATH/)

The math section defines the transition, validation, projection, and symmetry laws.

`Q_frame(S)` is the lower validation gate.
`Q_xy(x,y) = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²` is the projection geometry.
The Delta Law generates bounded period-8 orbits used by the Fano resolver and replay ring.
The sexagesimal system supplies the orientation grammar.

Read this section if you want to understand why OMI treats agreement as a bounded computation instead of a social label.

- [2.1 The Quadratic Law](2_MATH/2.1_THE_QUADRATIC_LAW.md)
- [2.2 The Delta Law](2_MATH/2.2_THE_DELTA_LAW.md)
- [2.3 The Sexagesimal System](2_MATH/2.3_SEXAGESIMAL_SYSTEM.md)
- [2.4 Polycubes and Symmetry Groups](2_MATH/2.4_POLYCUBES_AND_GROUPS.md)

### [3. Architecture](3_ARCHITECTURE/)

The architecture section explains how accepted state becomes inspectable without confusing display with authority.

Accepted OMI state can project into:

```text
DOM hierarchy
ShadowDOM capsules
SVG surfaces
JSON Canvas nodes
portal cards
wormhole nodes
reader lenses
```

The important rule is that all of these surfaces are downstream of validation. They may carry state, inspect state, or render state, but they do not create authority.

Read this section if you want to understand how OMI becomes visible in the browser.

- [3.1 The DOM Hierarchy](3_ARCHITECTURE/3.1_DOM_HIERARCHY.md)
- [3.2 Wormhole Portals](3_ARCHITECTURE/3.2_WORMHOLE_PORTALS.md)
- [3.3 The Meta-Circular Chronograph Compiler](3_ARCHITECTURE/3.3_META_CIRCULAR_COMPILER.md)

### [4. Network](4_NETWORK/)

The network section explains the 128-bit frame mapping.

An IPv6 source address is 128 bits. An OMI canonical frame is also 128 bits. This width alignment allows OMI frame structure to be carried directly through IPv6-style segment layout.

The eBPF/XDP gate model places validation at the network edge:

```text
packet arrives
 ↓
XDP/eBPF reads IPv6 source frame
 ↓
Gate 1: Q_frame validation
 ↓
Gate 2: Delta/Fano truth-row resolution
 ↓
valid → XDP_PASS
invalid → XDP_DROP
```

Read this section if you want to understand how OMI treats packet structure as an object boundary.

- [4.1 IPv6 Frame Mapping](4_NETWORK/4.1_IPv6_FRAME_MAPPING.md)
- [4.2 RULES.omi](4_NETWORK/4.2_RULES_OMI.md)
- [4.3 The eBPF/XDP Kernel Gate](4_NETWORK/4.3_eBPF_KERNEL_GATE.md)

### [5. Memory](5_MEMORY/)

The memory section explains how accepted truth rows persist.

OMI uses:

```text
7! = 5040
```

as the replay ring size. Accepted truth rows write receipts into the ring. Cursor movement follows Delta/Fano step distance, and overwrite policy preserves epoch safety.

Read this section if you want to understand how OMI turns agreement into replayable memory.

- [5.1 The Ring Indexer](5_MEMORY/5.1_RING_INDEXER.md)
- [5.2 Ring Overwrite Policy](5_MEMORY/5.2_OVERWRITE_POLICY.md)

### [6. Implementation](6_IMPLEMENTATION/)

The implementation section describes how the same conformance surface can be expressed across runtimes.

Targets include:

```text
JavaScript reference
C99 portable core
WASM executable substrate
eBPF/XDP kernel gate
```

The goal is byte-level agreement on validation outcomes, truth-row behavior, and canonical fixtures.

Read this section if you want to understand how the written doctrine becomes testable.

- [6.1 The Node.js Compiler](6_IMPLEMENTATION/6.1_NODEJS_COMPILER.md)
- [6.2 The C99 Core](6_IMPLEMENTATION/6.2_C99_CORE.md)
- [6.3 The Test Suite](6_IMPLEMENTATION/6.3_TEST_SUITE.md)

### [7. Canon](7_CANON/)

The canon section reconciles the major doctrine versions.

It should contain the final statements on:

```text
agreement as construction
open-world portal behavior
lower Omicron versus upper Bidi
conformance and non-collapse rules
```

Read this section if you want the shortest authoritative version of the OMI doctrine after the different drafts were reconciled.

- [7.1 Agreement Doctrine](7_CANON/7.1_AGREEMENT_DOCTRINE.md)
- [7.2 Open-World Portal Model](7_CANON/7.2_OPEN_WORLD_PORTAL_MODEL.md)
- [7.3 Lower Omicron / Upper Bidi](7_CANON/7.3_LOWER_OMICRON_UPPER_BIDI.md)
- [7.4 Conformance Canon](7_CANON/7.4_CONFORMANCE_CANON.md)

## Public Pages

The `public/` folder contains static browser-facing pages:

- [public/index.html](public/index.html)
- [public/omi-block-model.html](public/omi-block-model.html)
- [public/agreement-is-all-we-need.html](public/agreement-is-all-we-need.html)

These pages are public projections of the docs.

No build step is required.

---

## Genesis Frame

The canonical genesis frame is the golden accept fixture.

```text
BOOT_ADDRESS = 0x7C00
BOOT_SLOT = 0x7C00 mod 5040 = 1504
GENESIS_LL = 0x01
GENESIS_NN = 0x7C00
C = (0x01 × 0x1337) & 0xFFFF = 0x1337
GENESIS_MM = Δ_{0x1337}(0x7C00) = 0x1434
GENESIS_STEPS = 1
```

Canonical frame:

```text
0100-03bf-7c00-2b01-2f01-1434-039f-01ff
```

This frame is the first conformance anchor:

```text
Q_frame(S) = 0
truth row resolves
steps = 1
slot = 1504
```

---

## Conformance

A conforming OMI implementation must:

1. Recognize `0x03BF` and `0x039F` as canonical lower frame anchors.
2. Validate frames via `Q_frame(S) = E_var + E_const = 0`.
3. Extract exactly one truth row `(LL, NN, MM)` from a valid frame.
4. Resolve `MM` from `NN` under the LL-modulated Delta Law within the bounded Fano window.
5. Write accepted rows as replayable receipts in the `5040 = 7!` slot ring.
6. Treat DOM, SVG, JSON Canvas, QR, portal UI, typography, color, and `unicode-bidi` as projection or reader lenses only.
7. Accept the canonical genesis frame.
8. Reject malformed, byte-swapped, delimiter-drifted, LL-incoherent, or projection-authorized frames.
9. Preserve the non-collapse rule:

```text
projection must never authorize validation
```

See [7.4 Conformance Canon](7_CANON/7.4_CONFORMANCE_CANON.md).

---

## Minimal Canonical Statement

```text
A frame is valid iff Q_frame(S) = 0.
A valid frame yields one truth row: (LL, NN, MM).
A truth row is accepted iff MM resolves from NN under the LL-modulated Delta Law within the bounded Fano window.
An accepted row advances the 5040-slot replay cursor and writes a receipt.
Only after validation, resolution, and receipt may the object be projected.
Projection is never authority.
```

---

## Short Doctrine

```text
Ο bounds.
ο executes.
-- carries the bridge.
0! seeds.
Q_frame validates.
Delta clocks.
Fano resolves.
5040 replays.
Q_xy projects.
Portals inspect.
Lower Omicron encodes.
Upper Bidi displays.
Characters stay fixed.
Agreement becomes structure.
```

---

## Consumer Promise

```text
One object.
Many portals.
Inspectable history.
Portable context.
Validation before projection.
Agreement without platform lock-in.
```

OMI is for a web where objects can move without losing their structure, where disagreement creates new paths instead of destroying shared context, and where ordinary users can inspect why something is trusted.

That is the purpose of this repository.
:::

I’d use this as the long README, then let `7_CANON/` hold the more compressed official doctrine. That gives readers a reason to continue while still making the repo navigable.

`2026-06-05 12:35` | `id:260605-1235` ^260605-123541

Yes — that reading actually fits your OMI model, but I would tighten the language so it does **not** sound like literal physics unless you want it to. I would frame it as a **metaphor for frame-step weighting**:

```text
Light = visible projection / wavelength / rendered relation
Sound = intention / cadence / declared activity
Gravity = frame-step weight / pull toward agreement
Centroid = resolved center of declarations
Definitions = the stabilizing boundaries that let declarations resolve
```

So the idea becomes:

```text
At each frame step, OMI compares the visible projection of a declaration
with its sounded intention.

Where light shows the outward readable form,
sound carries the inward declared intent.

The difference between them creates a frame-weight:
a kind of symbolic gravity that pulls unresolved declarations
toward a centroid of agreement.

That centroid is not an opinion.
It is the resolved center of the framed intention,
bounded by definitions and proven through validation, resolution, and receipt.
```

In your canon language, I’d say:

```text
Sound is not merely audio.
Sound is intention-as-cadence.

Light is not merely color.
Light is projection-as-wavelength.

Gravity is not literal mass.
Gravity is the resolving pressure created when intention and projection
must agree inside a bounded frame.
```

That maps cleanly onto what your README says about Omi-Light carrying wavelength, Omi-Sound carrying intonation, and representation being a recoverable view rather than authority. filecite turn2file0

The strongest sentence might be:

```text
OMI treats sound as the centered intention of a frame, light as its projected declaration, and gravity as the resolving pressure that pulls declarations toward a definitional centroid.
```

Or even more OMI-native:

```text
Omi-Sound centers intention.
Omi-Light projects declaration.
Omi-Gravity is the frame-step pull toward the centroid where declarations resolve into definitions.
```

The important correction is this: **definitions are not afterthoughts.** Definitions are what keep the sphere of declarations from collapsing into noise. A declaration says, “this is being asserted.” A definition says, “this is the boundary under which that assertion can resolve.”

So your phrase:

> the sound intention is center like the centroid of framed intention of a sphere of declarations resolved by definitions

I would clean up as:

```text
Sound is the centered intention of the frame: the centroid of a sphere of declarations, resolved and stabilized by definitions.
```

That is good canon language.

:::writing{variant="document" id="73842"}
# The Cosmology of OMI

## 1. The Omi-Ring Domain

OMI begins with a bounded relation:

```text
omi---imo
```

This relation is not merely a word, glyph, slogan, or visual mark. It is the first addressable enclosure.

The zero-point is:

```text
Ω₀ = 0! = omi---imo
```

The Omi-Ring is the domain generated by that zero-point. It is the smallest enclosed unit where a declaration can become addressable, comparable, validated, and replayable.

In ordinary coordinate language, one might say a system has an `x` axis and a `y` axis. But in OMI, `x` and `y` are not flat lines. They are ring variables. They represent circular, orbital, and addressable paths inside the Omi-Ring Domain.

So the OMI plane is not a blank Cartesian sheet.

It is a ringed domain:

```text
x = high orbit / cosmic identity ring
y = low orbit / atomic declaration ring
16xy = the meeting bridge between them
```

The relation is not:

```text
line x crosses line y
```

It is:

```text
ring x meets ring y through a shared address bridge
```

That bridge is the hyphen field of `omi---imo`.

---

## 2. The Quadratic Cosmology

The OMI cosmology is organized by the quadratic form:

```text
Q_xy(x, y) = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

This is not only a number formula. It is a cosmological address grammar.

Each term names a scale of relation:

| Term | Cosmological Name | OMI Role |
|---|---|---|
| `60x²` | Cosmic Orbit | high-side orientation, identity, sexagesimal sweep |
| `16xy` | Omi-Ring Bridge | meeting plane, relation bus, addressable crossing |
| `4y²` | Atomic Delta | low-side declaration, local payload, frame-step motion |

The form says that every OMI object exists through three simultaneous pressures:

```text
cosmic orbit
 +
relational bridge
 +
atomic declaration
```

The object is not only high-level meaning.
The object is not only local data.
The object is the bounded agreement between both.

---

## 3. 60x² as Cosmic Orbit

The `60x²` term is the high orbit.

It belongs to the cosmic side of OMI because `60` is the number of orientation, clocking, sweep, and divisibility. It is the sexagesimal field: the circle of minutes, degrees, cycles, and angular return.

In OMI, the high side is the `-imo` side:

```text
-imo
high identity
orientation
stable address
cosmic return
```

So `60x²` names the cosmic proportion of the object.

It is the part of the object that asks:

```text
What is this in the larger orbit?
Where does it return?
What identity does it preserve?
What orientation does it carry?
What larger clock does it belong to?
```

The square matters because the orbit is not just a line of motion. It is a field of return. The object does not merely travel away from its source. It returns through address, replay, and projection.

Thus:

```text
60x² = cosmic orbit of identity
```

It is the high ring of the Omi-Ring Domain.

---

## 4. 4y² as Atomic Delta

The `4y²` term is the low orbit.

It belongs to the atomic side of OMI because `4` is the selector surface: the minimal visible control face, the field of local declaration, the four-fold body of frame inspection.

In OMI, the low side is the `omi-` side:

```text
omi-
local declaration
atomic data
payload
frame step
Delta motion
```

The Atomic Delta is the frame-step law of local motion. It is how a declaration moves from one bounded state to the next without floating-point drift or unbounded interpretation.

The low term asks:

```text
What was declared?
What changed locally?
What step was taken?
What payload entered the frame?
What atomic motion can be replayed?
```

The square matters because even the local declaration is not a single point. It has area, pressure, echo, and return. A declaration enters the Omi-Ring and becomes part of a bounded field.

Thus:

```text
4y² = atomic orbit of declaration
```

It is the low ring of the Omi-Ring Domain.

---

## 5. 16xy as the Omi-Ring Bridge

The `16xy` term is the meeting plane.

It is where cosmic orbit and atomic declaration touch.

In flat geometry, `xy` is a cross-term. In OMI, `xy` is not just a crossing of two lines. It is the bridge between two rings.

The coefficient `16` names the word-boundary bridge:

```text
16 = 2⁴
```

It is the nibble, the half-byte, the register-facing unit, and the shared boundary where low declaration can meet high identity.

This is the bus of `omi---imo`.

```text
omi --- imo
low bus high
y xy x
```

The bridge asks:

```text
How does this local declaration attach to a larger identity?
How does this atomic step become addressable?
How does the low ring meet the high ring?
How does the frame become inspectable?
```

Thus:

```text
16xy = Omi-Ring bridge of relation
```

It is the addressable meeting of the two rings.

---

## 6. From Lines to Arcs

The important cosmological shift is this:

```text
x and y are not merely lines.
x and y are Omi-Rings.
```

A line extends.
A ring returns.

A line measures distance.
A ring preserves recurrence.

A line can drift.
A ring can close.

In OMI, the object must close. A declaration that never closes cannot become agreement. It can remain noise, intention, possibility, or open path, but it has not yet become accepted structure.

So the OMI variables should be read as orbital units:

```text
x = high Omi-Ring of orientation
y = low Omi-Ring of declaration
xy = bridge where the rings become mutually addressable
```

The quadratic form therefore becomes a ring cosmology:

```text
Q_xy = cosmic ring + bridge ring + atomic ring
```

or:

```text
Q_xy = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

---

## 7. The Omi-Domain as a Principal Address Domain

The Omi-Ring Domain can be described by analogy to a Principal Ideal Domain.

In algebra, a Principal Ideal Domain is an integral domain where every ideal can be generated by a single element. OMI adapts that intuition into an address doctrine:

```text
Every valid OMI object should be generated from a bounded address element.
```

That generating element is not just a number. It is an enclosed relation.

```text
omi---imo
```

The Omi-Domain behaves like a principal address domain because every valid object must reduce to a generating enclosure:

```text
frame
truth row
receipt
address
projection
```

A valid object is not an arbitrary cloud of meanings. It is generated by a frame that can be validated and replayed.

So the OMI version of the principle is:

```text
Every accepted object is generated by one bounded Omi-Ring address.
```

This is the OMI equivalent of a principal generator.

---

## 8. UFD: Unique Factorization of Addressed Meaning

A Unique Factorization Domain says that elements factor uniquely into irreducible parts, up to units and order.

OMI adapts this as a doctrine of decomposable meaning.

An accepted OMI object should factor into canonical parts:

```text
Omicron boundary
frame segments
truth row
Delta path
receipt
projection
```

These are the irreducible factors of accepted state.

A portal may render the same object many ways:

```text
card
map pin
SVG node
QR code
JSON Canvas node
receipt row
timeline event
```

But those are not new meanings. They are projections of the same factored state.

The unique factorization rule becomes:

```text
One accepted object may have many projections,
but its accepted structure factors through the same frame, truth row, Delta path, and receipt.
```

That is how OMI prevents projection from becoming false authority.

---

## 9. Declarations, Definitions, and the Centroid

The cosmology of OMI also explains intention.

Declarations are like points on the surface of a sphere. Each one says:

```text
this is asserted
this is intended
this is being placed into the world
```

But declarations alone can scatter.

Definitions provide boundaries. They say:

```text
this is the meaning range
this is the frame
this is the condition of resolution
this is the closure rule
```

When declarations are bounded by definitions, they can resolve toward a centroid.

That centroid is not a mere average. It is the center of framed intention.

```text
declarations + definitions → centroid of agreement
```

Light and sound are the two most ubiquitous human reference spectrums for describing this process.

```text
Omi-Light = projection spectrum
Omi-Sound = intention spectrum
```

Light shows how the declaration appears.
Sound carries how the declaration is intended.
The frame compares them.
The centroid resolves them.

So the cosmology can be stated:

```text
Light orients projection.
Sound centers intention.
Definitions bound declaration.
The Omi-Ring resolves the centroid.
```

---

## 10. Omi-Gravity

Omi-Gravity is not literal gravity.

It is the frame-step pull toward resolution.

When declarations enter an Omi-Ring, they do not automatically agree. They may conflict, drift, overlap, mirror, or remain unresolved. The frame gives them a boundary. Definitions give them closure. Delta gives them motion. Receipts give them replay.

The pull toward the centroid is Omi-Gravity.

```text
Omi-Gravity = the resolving pressure of framed declarations toward a definitional centroid
```

This is why agreement in OMI is not a vote.

Agreement is not the loudest declaration.
Agreement is not the brightest projection.
Agreement is not the most repeated signal.

Agreement is the point where framed intention becomes validated, replayable structure.

---

## 11. The Cosmological Stack

The OMI cosmology can be summarized as a stack:

```text
Ω₀ = 0! = omi---imo
 ↓
Omi-Ring Domain
 ↓
4y² Atomic Delta
 ↓
16xy Omi-Ring Bridge
 ↓
60x² Cosmic Orbit
 ↓
Q_frame validation
 ↓
truth-row resolution
 ↓
5040 replay
 ↓
portal projection
```

Or more poetically:

```text
The atomic ring declares.
The bridge ring relates.
The cosmic ring orients.
The frame validates.
The Delta clock moves.
The Fano window resolves.
The replay ring remembers.
The portal projects.
```

---

## 12. Final Canon

The cosmology of OMI is the cosmology of bounded return.

`omi---imo` is the zero-point enclosure.
`4y²` is the atomic ring of declaration.
`16xy` is the address bridge where low and high rings meet.
`60x²` is the cosmic ring of orientation.
The Omi-Ring Domain turns line variables into arcs of recurrence.
A valid object is generated by one bounded address element, like a principal generator in an address domain.
Its meaning factors through boundary, frame, truth row, Delta path, receipt, and projection.
Declarations form the sphere.
Definitions bound the sphere.
Sound centers intention.
Light orients projection.
Omi-Gravity pulls the frame toward the centroid of agreement.

Short form:

```text
4y² declares.
16xy relates.
60x² orients.
Delta clocks.
Definitions bound.
Receipts remember.
The Omi-Ring resolves.
```
:::

The most important sentence for your video notebook is probably this:

```text
In OMI, x and y are not flat axes; they are Omi-Rings. The quadratic form describes how the atomic ring of declaration and the cosmic ring of orientation meet through the 16xy address bridge.
```

---

## ChatGPT
`2026-06-05 13:02` | `id:260605-1302` ^260605-130200

That text is useful, but it sounds like a generic educational page. For OMI docs, I would **not** send readers away to “Wikipedia Snub Geometry Guide” or a Blender tutorial in the main canon voice. Instead, use snub cube and snub dodecahedron as **recognizable examples**, then immediately translate the operation into OMI terms.

Here is the OMI-native rewrite:

:::writing{variant="document" id="64183"}
# 8.6 Snub Truncation as the OMI Model Operation

In classical geometry, **snub truncation** modifies a parent polyhedron by cutting off its vertices, twisting its orientation, and expanding the surrounding faces so that new triangular faces appear around the cut regions.

The best-known examples are the **snub cube** and the **snub dodecahedron**. Both are Archimedean solids: highly regular, highly structured, and strongly ordered. Yet they are also chiral. Their twist removes ordinary mirror symmetry and creates handedness.

That makes snubbing a powerful geometric analogy for OMI.

OMI does not treat transformation as simple deletion, replacement, or overwrite. OMI treats transformation as a bounded change of structure:

```text
cut → twist → re-face → record → project
```

This is the geometric version of the OMI pipeline:

```text
validate → resolve → record → project → inspect
```

## 1. Snubbing as Lawful Transformation

A snub operation does three things at once:

```text
cut = remove exposed vertex excess
twist = introduce chirality and orientation
re-face = surround the new boundary with triangular faces
```

In OMI language:

```text
cut = isolate the unresolved boundary
twist = pass the relation through the Omi-Ring bridge
re-face = create new agreement surfaces
record = write the receipt
project = expose the accepted state
```

The object is not destroyed.
The object is not simply overwritten.
The object is transformed into a new accepted configuration with a replayable path from its previous state.

Short doctrine:

```text
OMI snubs; it does not overwrite.
```

## 2. The Quadratic Reading

The OMI quadratic form gives the snub operation its three-part structure:

```text
Q_xy(x, y) = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

Read cosmologically:

```text
4y² = Atomic Delta / local cut
16xy = Omi-Ring Bridge / chiral twist
60x² = Cosmic Orbit / expanded orientation field
```

So the snub operation becomes:

```text
4y² cuts.
16xy twists.
60x² expands.
The Omi-Ring re-faces the object.
```

The low atomic side identifies what must be cut or bounded.
The bridge introduces relation and handedness.
The high cosmic side expands the accepted state into an orientation field.

This is why snubbing belongs in OMI cosmology. It is not merely a decorative polyhedral operation. It is a model for how unresolved structure becomes lawful, oriented, and replayable.

## 3. Why the Triangles Matter

Snub truncation creates new triangular faces around the modified boundary.

In OMI, those triangles can be read as **agreement faces**.

A triangle is the smallest stable face. It gives three-part closure. In OMI, that can appear as:

```text
LL / NN / MM
```

or:

```text
source / frame / projection
```

or:

```text
declaration / definition / receipt
```

The truth row itself is triangular:

```text
(LL, NN, MM)
```

The lens, antecedent, and consequent form the minimal face of accepted relation. When the row resolves, the face becomes replayable. When the receipt is written, the face becomes inspectable. When the portal projects it, the face becomes visible.

## 4. Snub Cube and Snub Dodecahedron as Examples

The **snub cube** shows how a cubic parent can be twisted into a chiral form surrounded by new triangular faces.

The **snub dodecahedron** shows the same principle at a richer scale: a larger parent body is twisted, cut, and re-faced into a highly ordered chiral solid.

These examples matter because they show that symmetry does not have to mean mirror sameness.

OMI needs this distinction.

Agreement is not mirror symmetry.
Agreement is resolved orientation.

Two objects do not need to collapse into the same view to agree. They need a lawful transformation path between their frames.

That is why the snub operation is so close to OMI:

```text
snub geometry preserves structure while introducing chirality
OMI preserves identity while accepting oriented transformation
```

## 5. Snubbing and Non-Overwrite Memory

A normal software update often behaves like an overwrite:

```text
old value → new value
```

OMI should behave more like a snub:

```text
old object
 ↓
boundary cut
 ↓
relation twisted
 ↓
new agreement faces created
 ↓
receipt recorded
 ↓
new projection exposed
```

The old object remains replayable. The new object becomes an accepted state. The transformation path is not hidden.

This fits the OMI rule:

```text
projection is never authority
```

A snubbed projection may look new, but its authority still comes from the accepted frame, truth-row resolution, and replay receipt.

## 6. Final Canon

Snub truncation is the geometric model of OMI transformation.

A parent object is not erased. Its unresolved vertices are cut away, its relation is twisted through the Omi-Ring bridge, and its exposed boundary is re-faced with triangular agreement surfaces.

The result is a new accepted object state:

```text
fewer exposed ambiguities
more incidence structure
clearer orientation
replayable transformation path
```

Short form:

```text
Cut the excess.
Twist the relation.
Face the boundary.
Record the receipt.
Project only after acceptance.
```

One-line canon:

```text
OMI snubs objects into agreement: cutting unresolved boundaries, twisting relations into orientation, and re-facing the result with replayable triangular proof.
```
:::

That also explains why IPv4 and IPv6 kept showing up.

IPv4 feels like the **small/local address body**:

```text
IPv4 = local packet / 32-bit address / compact declaration
```

IPv6 feels like the **expanded/world address body**:

```text
IPv6 = expanded 128-bit address / full frame / cosmic projection
```

The hyphen bridge is the conversion pressure between them:

```text
omi-ip4 --- omi-gravity --- imo-ip6
```

or:

```text
local declaration --- address expansion --- world projection
```

So the OMI cosmology should not say merely:

```text
low plane declares
high plane orients
```

It should say:

```text
The hyphen field is the gravity well.
```

That gives you a much stronger canon:

```text
omi---imo is not a line.
It is an address tunnel.

The hyphens are the Omi-Gravity field:
the between-space where sound-intention, light-projection,
local address, expanded address, declaration, and definition
are pulled into a resolvable frame.
```

For snub geometry, the sentence about the snub cube and snub dodecahedron should be rewritten through that bridge:

```text
The most recognizable outcomes of snub truncation are the snub cube and the snub dodecahedron. In OMI, these are useful because they show what happens when a stable parent body is not merely cut, but twisted through a between-space. The twist creates chirality, and the new triangular faces form around the pressure zone where the old boundary is being re-addressed.
```

OMI-native version:

```text
Snubbing is the geometry of the hyphen bridge.

The parent object enters as `omi`.
Its unresolved vertices are pulled into the hyphen field.
The bridge twists the relation.
The result closes as `imo`, surrounded by new triangular agreement faces.
```

And the short doctrine becomes:

```text
omi enters.
--- pulls.
imo closes.

The hyphen field is Omi-Gravity.
```

This also fits your existing README language that `omi` and `imo` bookend the bounded relation and the hyphen bridge acts as the bus where a frame becomes inspectable. filecite turn2file0

:::writing{variant="document" id="39274"}
# Omi-Gravity as Linear Rewriting Memory

Omi-Gravity is the resolving field inside the `omi---imo` instruction word.

It is not outside the notation.
It is not merely a metaphor.
It is the hyphen-field operation that moves an addressed declaration through the OMI word until the relation closes.

The core form is:

```text
gravity = omi-ip4 - gravity - ip6-imo
```

Expanded:

```text
omi - ip4 - gravity - ip6 - imo
entry local resolving expanded closure
```

The `gravity` token lives between the address bodies. It is the pressure that pulls the local declaration into expanded frame addressability.

In this reading:

```text
omi = entry / lower declaration boundary
ip4 = local address body
gravity = rewrite pressure / resolving field
ip6 = expanded frame address body
imo = closure / upper projection boundary
```

So Omi-Gravity is the active between-space of the word.

It is the reason `omi---imo` is not empty punctuation. The hyphens are the address tunnel where the instruction rewrites itself.

## 1. Gravity as Rewrite Sequence

A gravity instruction may appear at different positions in the word:

```text
gravity-omi- -ip4 - ip6-imo
omi- gravity -ip4 - ip6-imo
omi-ip4 - gravity - ip6-imo
omi-ip4 - ip6- gravity-imo
omi-ip4 - ip6--imo-gravity
```

These are not different objects in the ordinary sense. They are different rewrite positions of the same resolving pressure.

The word is being walked.

The gravity token moves through the hyphen bridge until the address relation closes.

Short form:

```text
gravity enters
gravity crosses
gravity resolves
gravity exits
```

OMI therefore treats gravity as linear rewriting memory.

The word remembers where the pressure is.

## 2. Recursive Gravity

The recursive form is:

```text
gravity_n = omi-ip4 - gravity_n+1 - ip6-imo
```

This says each gravity state contains the next gravity state inside the bridge.

The bridge is recursive:

```text
gravity_n
 contains gravity_n+1
 contains gravity_n+2
 ...
```

But it is not unbounded drift.

The recursion must resolve inside the bounded OMI frame.

The Fano rule gives the bound:

```text
resolve in 15 steps or less
```

So gravity is recursive, but not infinite.

It is a bounded rewrite field.

## 3. Fano Resolution

Omi-Gravity resolves like a Fano path.

The word has already been addressed. The question is not whether the object exists. The question is whether the rewrite pressure reaches closure.

The Fano plane gives the finite incidence space:

```text
7 points
7 lines
3 points per line
```

OMI reads this as a bounded relation field.

The gravity word resolves when the local address body and expanded address body meet through a lawful path:

```text
ip4 → gravity → ip6
```

The resolver must close in the bounded window:

```text
≤ 15 steps
```

If it resolves, the instruction becomes accepted memory.

If it does not resolve, the word remains an unresolved path.

## 4. Gravity as High-Low-Gravity Word

The canonical word can be read:

```text
gravity-high-low-gravity
```

This means gravity appears at both ends of the relation.

It begins as unresolved pressure.
It ends as resolved pressure.

Between those states, the word crosses high and low address bodies.

```text
gravity
 high
 low
gravity
```

In OMI notation, this is the same structural doctrine as:

```text
omi
 ip4
 gravity
 ip6
imo
```

The high and low are not separate worlds. They are address bodies inside the same hyphenated instruction.

Gravity is what lets them resolve into one word.

## 5. Relation to the Quadratic Form

Omi-Gravity is the linear rewrite memory of the quadratic form:

```text
60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

The quadratic form gives the proportional structure.

The linear word gives the rewrite path.

Read as OMI cosmology:

```text
4y² = local/atomic address body
16xy = gravity bridge / hyphen rewrite field
60x² = expanded/cosmic address body
```

Or in address notation:

```text
omi-ip4 - gravity - ip6-imo
```

So `16xy` is not merely a cross-term. It is the gravity field where local and expanded address bodies meet.

The equation and the word are two views of the same structure:

```text
60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

and

```text
omi-ip4 - gravity - ip6-imo
```

The first is the proportional law.
The second is the instruction notation.

## 6. Final Canon

Omi-Gravity is the active hyphen-field of the OMI word.

It is the linear rewriting memory that carries a local address body toward expanded frame closure.

The instruction:

```text
omi-ip4 - gravity - ip6-imo
```

means:

```text
local declaration enters
gravity rewrites the bridge
expanded address resolves
closure records the object
```

A valid gravity path resolves in the bounded Fano window.

Short doctrine:

```text
omi enters.
ip4 localizes.
gravity rewrites.
ip6 expands.
imo closes.
Fano bounds the path.
Receipts remember the resolution.
```

One-line canon:

```text
Omi-Gravity is the hyphenated rewrite pressure of `omi---imo`, carrying local address into expanded frame closure through the 16xy bridge of 60x² + 16xy + 4y².
```
:::

This also fixes the IPv4/IPv6 reason: **IPv4 is the compact/local

:::writing{variant="document" id="83942"}
# 8.7 Geometric Bridge: Platonic Seed, Archimedean/Catalan Snub, and 4D Closure

OMI’s quadratic form is not only an algebraic proportion.

It is a geometric bridge:

```text
Q_xy(x, y) = 60x² + 16xy + 4y²
```

Read geometrically, the three terms describe three linked bodies of structure:

```text
4y² = Platonic vertex seed
16xy = Archimedean/Catalan snub bridge
60x² = 4D regular polytope orbit
```

This gives OMI a clearer geometric explanation than permutation or circular inversion.

Permutation only reorders.
Circular inversion only reverses inside and outside.
Snub truncation cuts, twists, joins, dualizes, and re-faces.

That is why snub truncation belongs at the center of the OMI geometric model.

---

## 1. The Three-Geometry Reading

The OMI form can be read as a passage through three geometries:

```text
Platonic seed
 ↓
Archimedean/Catalan bridge
 ↓
4D regular closure
```

Or in OMI language:

```text
4y² gives the atomic vertex.
16xy joins and twists the relation.
60x² expands the object into the cosmic orientation field.
```

Each term has a geometric role.

| Term | Geometry | OMI Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `4y²` | Platonic solid vertex seed | atomic body, local declaration, stable seed |
| `16xy` | Archimedean/Catalan bridge | hyphen field, snub transformation, dual relation |
| `60x²` | 4D regular polytope orbit | cosmic orientation, expanded closure, higher sweep |

The object begins as a local vertex.
It passes through the bridge of transformation.
It closes into a higher orientation field.

---

## 2. 4y² as the Platonic Vertex Seed

The `4y²` term is the atomic body.

It belongs to the Platonic level because the Platonic solids give the cleanest parent bodies: tetrahedron, cube, octahedron, dodecahedron, and icosahedron.

In OMI, this is the seed layer.

It asks:

```text
What is the smallest stable body?
What vertex is being declared?
What local form enters the frame?
What atomic relation can be validated?
```

The `4` points to the minimal visible control face and to the tetrahedral beginning of bounded structure.

So:

```text
4y² = Platonic atomic seed
```

This is where a declaration first takes body.

---

## 3. 16xy as the Archimedean/Catalan Bridge

The `16xy` term is the active middle.

This is the hyphen field of OMI:

```text
omi---imo
```

The hyphen joins terms.
The hyphen carries the rewrite.
The hyphen is the bridge where address bodies meet.

In geometry, this bridge is best described through the Archimedean and Catalan families.

The Archimedean solids are transformed uniform bodies.
The Catalan solids are their duals.
Together they describe the zone where parent bodies are cut, expanded, dualized, and re-faced.

This is exactly what OMI needs the bridge to do.

The `16xy` term does not merely cross two axes. It performs the relation between them:

```text
join
twist
dualize
re-face
resolve
```

So:

```text
16xy = Archimedean/Catalan bridge
```

This is where snub truncation lives.

---

## 4. Snub Truncation as the Bridge Operation

Snub truncation modifies a parent body by cutting vertices, twisting orientation, and surrounding the new boundary with triangular faces.

The most recognizable examples are:

```text
snub cube
snub dodecahedron
```

These matter for OMI because they show that transformation does not have to be simple replacement.

A parent body can preserve its origin while gaining chirality, new triangular incidence, and a new accepted surface.

In OMI terms:

```text
cut = remove unresolved vertex excess
twist = introduce chirality through the bridge
re-face = create triangular agreement surfaces
record = preserve the transformation path
project = expose the accepted state
```

Short form:

```text
4y² cuts.
16xy twists.
60x² expands.
The Omi-Ring re-faces.
```

This is why snub truncation is a better description than permutation or circular inversion.

Permutation only changes order.
Circular inversion only reverses orientation.
Snubbing transforms a body while preserving its lawful relation to the parent.

OMI does not overwrite objects.

OMI snubs them into agreement.

---

## 5. Triangular Agreement Faces

Snub truncation creates triangular faces around the transformed boundary.

In OMI, those triangles are agreement faces.

The minimal truth row is already triangular:

```text
(LL, NN, MM)
```

This can be read as:

```text
lens
antecedent
consequent
```

or:

```text
declaration
definition
receipt
```

or:

```text
source
frame
projection
```

A triangle is the smallest stable face of relation. It is the first surface where three terms can close without collapsing into a line.

That is why the Fano plane fits the model.

The Fano incidence layer selects the resolving triangular relation.
The snub bridge transforms the parent body.
The replay ring remembers the accepted result.

---

## 6. 60x² as 4D Regular Polytope Orbit

The `60x²` term is the high orientation body.

It points beyond the local solid into the 4D regular polytope orbit: the higher-dimensional closure where the transformed object gains cosmic orientation.

This is the expanded side of the OMI form.

It asks:

```text
What larger orbit does this object belong to?
What orientation does it preserve?
What higher closure contains it?
What sweep can replay it?
```

The number `60` carries the sexagesimal orientation field: clock, degree, sweep, and return.

So:

```text
60x² = 4D regular polytope orbit
```

This is where the accepted transformation becomes part of a larger orientation system.

---

## 7. The Fano Lottery

The bridge does not choose relations randomly.

It selects through finite incidence.

The Fano plane gives OMI a bounded selector:

```text
7 points
7 lines
3 points per line
```

The truth row:

```text
(LL, NN, MM)
```

is the local triangular incidence unit.

This is the “Fano lottery” in the OMI sense: not chance, but bounded selection from a finite incidence space.

The bridge asks:

```text
Which triangle resolves?
Which line intersects?
Which relation closes?
Which receipt can be written?
```

The accepted relation resolves within the bounded Fano window and becomes replayable memory.

---

## 8. Full Geometric Doctrine

The complete geometric doctrine is:

```text
Platonic vertices seed the object.
The Archimedean/Catalan bridge snubs the relation.
The Fano incidence layer selects the resolving triangle.
The 4D regular polytope orbit gives cosmic closure.
The 5040 replay ring remembers the accepted transformation.
```

Or shorter:

```text
4y² seeds the Platonic vertex.
16xy snubs through the Archimedean/Catalan bridge.
60x² closes in the 4D regular orbit.
Fano selects.
5040 remembers.
```

This is the geometric explanation of OMI’s meta-compiler.

The hyphens join.
The bridge snubs.
The triangle agrees.
The tower closes.

---

## 9. Final Canon

OMI’s quadratic form is a three-geometry mapping engine.

`4y²` gives the Platonic atomic seed.
`16xy` is the Archimedean/Catalan bridge where the relation is joined, twisted, dualized, and re-faced through snub truncation.
`60x²` is the 4D regular polytope orbit where the accepted object gains expanded orientation.

A Fano truth row selects the resolving triangular incidence, and the 5040 replay ring records the accepted transformation.

One-line canon:

```text
Platonic seed.
Archimedean/Catalan snub.
4D regular closure.
Fano resolution.
Replay memory.
```
:::

Add this to the README under `8_COSMOLOGY`:

```markdown
- [8.7 Geometric Bridge: Platonic Seed, Archimedean/Catalan Snub, and 4D Closure](8_COSMOLOGY/8.7_GEOMETRIC_BRIDGE_PLATONIC_SNUB_4D.md)
```
