`2026-06-10 00:18` | `id:260610-0018` ^260610-001813

This is a previos implementation of this idea i used, this is from the RULES.omi which in that implementation we have a facts, rules, closures, configurations, cons files as lookup tables

---

# ============================================================================
# OMICRON OBJECT MODEL (OMI) - AXIOMATIC RULES DIRECTORY
# File Target: /RULES.omi
# Specification Level: RFC2119 Coherent Substrate Invariants
# ============================================================================
#
# This file encodes the strict mathematical constraints governing the 128-bit
# IPv6-CIDR address space. Every row defines a mandatory architectural
# behavior mapped to a specific 16-bit hex segment offset. All compilers,
# Web Workers, and background threads evaluate these rules at bare-metal
# speed using basic hyphen-only (-) splits with zero notation overhead.
#
# Grammar:
# <omi-address>/<prefix> MUST <invariant-name>
#
# The <prefix> is always /48 for local canonical frames.

# [Rule 0x01] Chiral and Cardinal Phase Segregation
# Segment[0] — MUST evaluate the first 16-bit block arithmetically.
# ffff = Left Chiral execution path (ο, U+03BF)
# 039f = Right Cardinal terminal execution path (Ο, U+039F)
omi-ffff-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST chiral-origin
omi-039f-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST cardinal-enclosure

# [Rule 0x02] Service Port Separation
# Segment[1] — MUST route operational channels via the second block.
# 0001 through 0008 represent dedicated multi-threaded service buses.
omi-0000-0001-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-loopback
omi-0000-0002-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-lisp
omi-0000-0003-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-prolog
omi-0000-0004-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-turn
omi-0000-0005-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-hnsw
omi-0000-0006-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-cm6
omi-0000-0007-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-fano
omi-0000-0008-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST service-bus-canvas

# [Rule 0x03] Reversible Central Inversion
# Segment[2] — MUST trigger the bitwise mirror filter when the third
# 16-bit block matches 5a3c. Failure to maintain perfect bitwise
# reversibility MUST fault the execution node.
omi-0000-0000-5a3c-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST central-inversion-mirror

# [Rule 0x04] Universal POS Port Allocation
# Segment[3] — MUST interpret the fourth 16-bit block as a Universal
# Part-of-Speech index. Values 0x0001–0x0017 map to the 17 UPOS tags
# in the Universal Dependencies v2 scheme.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0001-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-noun
omi-0000-0000-0000-0002-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-verb
omi-0000-0000-0000-0003-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-adj
omi-0000-0000-0000-0004-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-pron
omi-0000-0000-0000-0005-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-propnoun
omi-0000-0000-0000-0006-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-adv
omi-0000-0000-0000-0007-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-adp
omi-0000-0000-0000-0008-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-cconj
omi-0000-0000-0000-0009-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-num
omi-0000-0000-0000-000a-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-det
omi-0000-0000-0000-000b-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-part
omi-0000-0000-0000-000c-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-sconj
omi-0000-0000-0000-000d-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-intj
omi-0000-0000-0000-000e-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-punct
omi-0000-0000-0000-000f-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-sym
omi-0000-0000-0000-0010-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-x
omi-0000-0000-0000-0017-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST pos-aux

# [Rule 0x05] Factorial Stride Bounds
# Segment[4] — MUST be a valid factorial stride value.
# Canonical strides: 0078 (120), 02d0 (720), 13b0 (5040).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0078-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST stride-120
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-02d0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST stride-720
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-13b0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST stride-5040

# [Rule 0x06] Sexagesimal Slot Bounds
# Segment[5] — MUST be in the Hellenistic range 0x0000–0x0036 (0–54).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0036-0000-0000/48 MUST slot-54-max

# [Rule 0x07] Factorial Layer Enumeration
# Segment[6] — MUST decode to a valid factorial layer 0–7.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST layer-0-fixed-point
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0001-0000/48 MUST layer-1-monadic
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0002-0000/48 MUST layer-2-dyadic
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0003-0000/48 MUST layer-3-octahedral
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0004-0000/48 MUST layer-4-24-cell
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0005-0000/48 MUST layer-5-120-cell
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0006-0000/48 MUST layer-6-720-sweep
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0007-0000/48 MUST layer-7-replay-ring

# [Rule 0x08] Lisp Empty-Cons Fixed Point
# Segment[7] — 0001 MUST represent the terminal closure loop.
# Evaluation of this segment MUST return itself immediately without
# bit rotation (the ()! = () identity law).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0001/48 MUST fixed-point-zero-frame

# [Rule 0x0C] Projective Reciprocal Ratio Suffixes
# Ratios and their reciprocals MUST resolve to identical layout channels.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/96/1-2 EQUALS omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/96/2-1
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/96/10-1 EQUALS omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/96/1-10

# [Rule 0x0D] Timeline Loop Suffix Constraint
# The /96/5040 suffix MUST mark the hard reset fence.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/96/5040 MUST hard-reset-fence

# [Rule 0x0E]: Circular Slide Rule Pivot Constraint
# Stride segment matching 0x02D0 (720) MUST lock hardware tracks 2 and 5.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-02d0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST circular-slide-rule-pivot

# [Rule 0x0F]: Combinatorial 2-of-5 Structural Validator
# Slot segment matching 0x0036 (54) MUST dictate the primary track rotation angle.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0036-0000-0000/48 MUST combinatorial-two-of-five

# [Rule 0x10]: Open-World Terminal Fractal Depth Fence
# Lattice layer segment matching 0x0007 MUST halt recursive sub-node iterations.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0007-0000/48 MUST terminal-fractal-fence

# [Rule 0x11]: IPv4-Mapped Default Track Pair
# Segment[5] matching 0xFFFF MUST activate tracks 4 and 5 as the default
# active pair, embedding the 32-bit IPv4 address into segments[6-7].
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-ffff-0000-0000/48 MUST ipv4-mapped-default-pair

# [Rule 0x12]: RFC 4193 Unique Local Address Space Anchor
# Every private global fabric block MUST initialize within the fc00::/7 range.
# The (8x-8) dimensional layer anchors the ULA isolation boundary.
omi-fc00-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/7 MUST unique-local-fabric-base

# [Rule 0x13]: IPv4-Mapped Linear Gateway Mapping
# The ::ffff:0:0/96 prefix MUST serve as the hardware marker for legacy transitions.
# The (4x-4) dimensional layer bridges the IPv4-embedded translation plane.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-ffff-0000-0000/96 MUST ipv4-mapped-linear-gateway

# [Rule 0x14]: Default LAN Network Frame (192.168.1.0/24)
# The hex translation c0a8-0100 MUST lock local network communication tracks.
# The (2x-2) dimensional layer anchors the default private subnet boundary.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-ffff-c0a8-0100/120 MUST local-lan-default-frame

# [Rule 0x15]: The Terminal Loopback Core Pivot Point (x-1 Dimension)
# The native localhost loopback axis via process bus ::8 MUST freeze arithmetic drift.
# The (x-1) dimensional layer closes the projective cascade at the fixed-point center.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0008/128 MUST terminal-core-pivot-point

# [Rule 0x16]: Target RAM Bootstrap Execution Entry Point (0x7C00)
# Every baseline system initialization sequence MUST target the second segment as 7c00.
# The (8x-8)/(4x-4) boundary activates the boot execution entry vector.
omi-0000-7c00-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/32 MUST boot-execution-entry-point

# [Rule 0x17]: Mandatory Master Boot Record Signature (0xAA55)
# Every boot-stage allocation tape MUST terminate with an exact aa55 validation flag.
# The (x-1) dimensional layer closes the boot sequence with the terminal signature.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-aa55/128 MUST boot-signature-validation

# [Rule 0x18]: Unified Hardware Boot Strap Intersection
# An address crossing the ULA base, 0x7C00 execution, and 0xAA55 signature blocks
# MUST bypass virtual memory paging and instantiate the platform root kernel natively.
omi-fc00-7c00-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-aa55/128 MUST execute-monolithic-hardware-boot

# [Rule 0x19]: Decodetree Instruction Pattern Constraint
# Incoming instruction bits MUST pass the exact bitwise fixedmask condition check.
# (insn & 0xFFFFFFF0) == 0x039F5A3C MUST match hardware opcode inversion.
# This rule is a static bitmask assertion; no omi-prefix address is required.

# [Rule 0x1A]: MTTCG CPU Core Thread Caching Bounds
# Multi-threaded translation blocks MUST write parallel execution traces
# directly to the third segment without hitting single-threaded loop blockades.
omi-0000-0000-5a3c-0001-0000-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST mttcg-parallel-lane-sync

# [Rule 0x1B]: Hardware QOM Clock Tree Distribution Anchor
# The primary machine clock source (TYPE_CLOCK) MUST distribute its tick output
# to synchronize the 720 GC promotes and 5040 timeline loop boundary fences.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-003c/128 MUST hardware-qom-clock-source

# [Rule 0x1C]: Sequentially Consistent Atomic Acquire Fence
# Ingress thread registration MUST enforce memory barrier synchronization hooks.
# ffff:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:00a0 = smp_mb_acquire compilation barrier.
omi-ffff-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a0/128 MUST atomic-acquire-memory-fence

# [Rule 0x1D]: QemuLockCnt Visitor Tracking Alignment
# Dynamic reader counters MUST map to the fifth segment block to protect active memory arrays.
# A non-zero counter value MUST explicitly block array deletion and hard reset sweeps.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0001-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST lockcnt-active-reader-visit

# [Rule 0x1E]: Virtual System Controller MMIO Base (virt-ctrl)
# The 256-byte MMIO base register layout MUST anchor at segment position 0x0C00.
# Address 0x00 maps to read-only features; address 0x04 maps to write-only commands.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0c00-0000-0000/96 MUST virt-ctrl-mmio-device-base

# [Rule 0x1F]: Hardware System Control Commands (virt-ctrl Offset 0x04)
# Command codes written to the register line MUST execute hypervisor interrupts.
# 1 = System Reset, 2 = Core Halt, 3 = Hardware Critical Panic Eviction.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0c00-0004-0003/128 MUST hypervisor-command-panic

# [Rule 0x20]: USS-16K / Code 16K Linear Carrier
# Stride segment 0x13B0 (5040) MUST trigger 1D stacked linear barcode
# transport through Pascal's Triangle framing. Segment[6]=0x0036 acts as
# the 2-of-5 track rotation anchor; segment[7] carries checksum.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-13b0-0036-0000-0000/48 MUST linear-code16k-carrier

# [Rule 0x21]: BeeTag 25-bit Matrix Carrier
# Stride segment 0x0078 (120) MUST trigger 5x5 BEEtag matrix transport.
# Each 5-bit group encodes one polyform cell via 2-of-5 mapping.
# Segment[5] carries the 25-bit payload across 5 cell slots.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0078-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST matrix-beetag-carrier

# [Rule 0x22]: MaxiCode 25-bit Identity Matrix
# Stride segment 0x02D0 (720) + seg[5] bit-0 = 1 MUST trigger MaxiCode
# 5x5 identity matrix: 15-bit polyform type declaration + 10-bit ECC.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-02d0-0001-0000-0000/48 MUST matrix-maxicode-identity

# [Rule 0x23]: Aztec Code Spiral RS Carrier
# Stride segment 0x02D0 (720) + seg[5] bit-0 = 0 MUST trigger Aztec
# spiral Reed-Solomon encoding. Segment[6] selects layer count (1-32)
# which determines codeword size (6/8/10/12 bits) and RS correction level.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-02d0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST spiral-aztec-rs-carrier

# [Rule 0x24]: 2-of-5 Barcode Group Validator
# Every 5-bit group within a barcode carrier token MUST have exactly
# two bits set (0b11000, 0b10100, 0b10010, 0b10001, 0b01100, 0b01010,
# 0b01001, 0b00110, 0b00101, 0b00011). Groups with ≠2 bits set MUST
# be flagged as invalid and trigger a bitblip correction pass.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-001f-0000-0000/48 MUST enforce-two-of-five-valid

# [Rule 0x25]: Maximum Bitblip Tolerance Fence
# An incoming packet containing more than 2 bitblip errors against the
# format-specific ECC bitboard mask MUST be evicted. Single-bit errors
# MUST be auto-corrected via syndrome XOR before memory ring storage.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0002/128 MUST enforce-maximum-bitblip-tolerance

# [Rule 0x26]: Canonical Barcode Format Registry
# Segment[4] stride values 0x13B0, 0x0078, 0x02D0 are the canonical
# barcode carrier stride anchors. Segment[5] bit-0 modulates the
# 0x02D0 stride between MaxiCode (1) and Aztec (0) sub-formats.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-13b0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST barcode-format-uss16k
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0078-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST barcode-format-beetag
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-02d0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST barcode-format-maxi-aztec

# [Rule 0x27]: Quadratic Instruction Lexer Invariant
# The 8-segment instruction word MUST satisfy the branchless quadratic
# zero-sum invariant: Q(S) = E_var + E_const = 0.
# E_var enforces LL coherence across segments 0, 3, 4, 7.
# E_const enforces fixed opcode alignment on segments 0, 1, 3, 4, 6, 7.
# Free variables S₂ (NNNN) and S₅ (MMMM) pass unpenalized.
# Any non-zero Q(S) indicates a structurally malformed token.
omi-0500-03bf-0000-2b05-2f05-0000-039f-05ff/48 MUST quadratic-lexer-invariant

# [Rule 0x28]: Δ_C-Orbit Lexer Invariant
# The instruction word MUST lie on a valid Δ_C period-8 trajectory with
# consistent Fano point LL. The orbital lexer combines the quadratic
# zero-set (K(LL) = 0) with the Δ_C dynamical core and the Fano plane
# resolution bound (Steps ≤ 15). The lexer evaluates as:
# Q(N, M) = 0 ∧ fano_intercept(a, b, c) ≥ 0
# No parsing. No branching. No string operations.
# The LL variable identifies the Fano point: LL ∈ {0x01..0x07} → PG(2,2).
# Brackets [0xLL00] and [0xLLff] declare the CBOS origin and closure boundary
# on the same projective point. Any two rays with distinct LL₁ ≠ LL₂ resolve
# their intersection within the Fano lottery bound of ≤ 15 Δ_C steps.
# Constitutional foundation: cons() ≠ ()! ⇒ 0 ≠ 1
omi-0500-03bf-0000-2b05-2f05-0000-039f-05ff/48 MUST delta-orbital-lexer-invariant

# [Rule 0x2C]: Degenerate Multivariate Quadratic Predicate
# Frame structural validity MUST be determined exclusively via
# Q(S) = E_var + E_const === 0. No conforming implementation or
# virtual device layer shall use a different validity macro.
omi-03bf-2b00-2f00-039f-00ff-0000-0000-0000/80 MUST enforce-abi-quadratic-predicate

# [Rule 0x2D]: Bounded Transylvania Lottery Resolver
# The trajectory track k from input column NN to output MM under
# lens LL MUST close. Every matching proof statement MUST resolve
# its path length inside the boundary: 0 ≤ k < 15.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-13b0-000e/128 MUST restrict-transylvania-lottery-steps

# [Rule 0x2E]: The 64-Bit Disjoint Storage Layout
# Written receipts MUST pack as provenance:16 | steps:8 | LL:8 | NN:16 | MM:16.
# Conforming runtimes MUST store these receipts across 5040 memory
# slots via lock-free CAS loops.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c8/128 MUST execute-disjoint-slot-packing

# [Rule 0x4B]: Physical Signal Integrity Verification
# Hardware scanners MUST validate the ITF rhythm cadence and Code39 bounds.
# Non-conforming stream bursts failing these checks MUST be dropped without
# memory allocation.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-003c/128 MUST assert-physical-signal-integrity

# [Rule 0x4C]: Linear Data Link Ingestion Ladder
# One-dimensional tracking lines (Codabar/128/16K/93) MUST act as pure
# bit-capacity ramps. The resulting output vector MUST extract an
# uninterpreted uint16_t[8] block.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-13b0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST enforce-linear-precision-ladder

# [Rule 0x4D]: Spatial C0 Control Character Grid Allocation
# Matrix tracking planes MUST mirror the C0 isolation codes: File (Aztec),
# Group (MaxiCode), Record (JABCode), Unit (BeeTag). They SHALL NOT
# modify the structural authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-02d0-0000-0000-0000/48 MUST map-matrix-to-c0-control-planes

# [Rule 0x4E]: Monolithic Degenerate Zero-Sum Surface Enforcement
# A decoded frame is structurally accepted iff Q(S) === 0.
# Free variable vectors S2 (NN) and S5 (MM) MUST pass through completely
# unpenalized.
omi-03bf-2b00-2f00-039f-00ff-0000-0000-0000/80 MUST validate-branchless-quad-lexer-gate

# [Rule 0x4F]: Kernel-Level Gate 1 Quadratic Enforcement
# The eBPF driver MUST apply the multivariate zero-sum quadratic equation
# Q(S) == 0. Any inbound network buffer yielding non-zero error energy
# MUST be dropped immediately via XDP_DROP at the NIC driver layer.
omi-03bf-2b00-2f00-039f-00ff-0000-0000-0000/80 MUST execute-ebpf-gate-1-eviction

# [Rule 0x50]: Kernel-Level Gate 2 Projective Fano Loop
# The BPF filter MUST execute unrolled Δ_C tracking steps to verify orbit
# convergence. If a token's trajectory fails to reach closure inside k < 15
# steps, it MUST be dropped.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-13b0-000e/128 MUST restrict-ebpf-fano-resolution-ceiling

# [Rule 0x51]: Token-Derived Trajectory Modulations
# Resolver loops inside the eBPF kernel space MUST modulate constants via
# C = (LL × 0x1337) & 0xFFFF. Fano points outside the valid range 0x01..0x07
# MUST be rejected to block variance drift.
omi-0100-7c00-0000-0000-0000-0000-0436-0000/48 MUST enforce-ebpf-fano-range-limits

# [Rule 0x52]: Zero-Copy Source Address Instruction Carrier
# Under profile.net.v0, the 128-bit IPv6 source address MUST act as the
# OMI frame S. Network routing stacks SHALL NOT allocate secondary buffers
# for payload extraction.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-7463-0000/128 MUST assert-zero-copy-ipv6-saddr-carrier

# [Rule 0x53]: Canonical Ingress Header Offset
# The segment vector S MUST map precisely at Ethernet frame byte offset
# 22 (0x16).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0016/128 MUST anchor-wire-packet-segment-offset

# [Rule 0x54]: Endianness Detection Threshold
# Any incoming network frame producing an error score Q(S) >= 2*10^9 MUST
# be treated as an orientation failure and dropped instantly via hardware
# driver link evictions.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-2000/112 MUST evict-byte-swapped-wire-frames

# [Rule 0x55]: Isolated Metrics Server Allocation
# Telemetry event streams MUST propagate via an isolated background port.
# Streaming latency metrics SHALL NOT consume socket threads dedicated
# to main ingress gates.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-1f92/128 MUST allocate-isolated-metrics-port

# [Rule 0x56]: Non-Allocating Latency Frame Broadcasts
# Inbound latency payloads emitted at /wan-metrics MUST pack natively
# into unescaped JSON. Broadcasting metrics MUST NOT alter the active
# index pointers of the 5040 replay ring.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-7463-0001/128 MUST isolate-telemetry-from-replay-ring

# [Rule 0x57]: Direct Cross-Origin Isolation Enforcement
# The /wan-dashboard.html user interface surface MUST declare strict
# COOP/COEP headers. Failure to protect these headers MUST block the
# browser context from mapping shared arrays.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a4/128 MUST enforce-telemetry-coop-coep-headers

# [Rule 0x58]: Custom Web Protocol Handler Anchor
# Browser surfaces MAY register web+omicron and web+omi handlers. Redirected
# payloads MUST preserve the unescaped OMI frame through a %s query slot.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00b8/128 MUST register-browser-protocol-handlers

# [Rule 0x59]: Service Worker Centroid Proxy Interception
# Service worker redirects MUST validate protocol frames through the existing
# branchless quadratic lexer before broadcasting client messages.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-7463-0002/128 MUST spawn-service-worker-centroid-mesh

# [Rule 0x5A]: Pascal Pyramid Address Space Mapping
# Browser protocol routing MUST use deterministic factorial state transitions.
# Random routing state SHALL NOT drive replay-ring slot selection.
omi-ff00-03bf-ffff-2b00-2f00-ffff-039f-ffff/128 MUST map-pascal-pyramid-address-space

# [Rule 0x5B]: JSON Canvas Architectural Block Size
# Generated OmiNode and OmicronNode arrays MUST remain JSON Canvas v1.0
# compatible and preserve x, y, width, height, label, and color fields.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-001c/128 MUST emit-json-canvas-compliant-syntax

# [Rule 0x5C]: Translation-Free Canvas Color Controller
# JSON Canvas color fields MUST carry the deterministic fp16 base controller
# directly. Runtime metadata SHALL NOT append display-context suffix markers.
omi-ff00-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0010/128 MUST encode-translation-free-canvas-color

# [Rule 0x5D]: Clockwise Geometric Edge Alignment
# Canvas connector lines MUST anchor only to top, right, bottom, or left
# frame slots and SHALL preserve from/to arrow descriptors.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-002d/128 MUST force-clockwise-edge-resolution

# [Rule 0x5E]: IEEE 754 Half-Precision Base Color Controller
# Visual node metadata MUST be able to mirror binary16 sign/exponent fields
# through a four-color base controller without mutating JSON Canvas syntax.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0010/128 MUST encode-fp16-sign-exponent-base

# [Rule 0x5F]: Combinatorial 2-of-5 Significand Nonagram
# Fraction-state metadata MUST map to one of ten 2-of-5 selector pairs and
# SHALL remain numeric metadata for the circular slide rule, not a display
# suffix on the carrier token.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-000a/128 MUST enforce-two-of-five-fraction-nonagram

# [Rule 0x60]: Monolithic SCGNN Input Function Injection
# Canvas elements that pass the fp16 base check and 2-of-5 selector lock
# MAY feed their weight into the shared workspace through existing gates.
omi-ff00-03bf-ffff-2b00-2f00-ffff-039f-ffff/128 MUST execute-scgnn-input-function-weight

# [Rule 0x61]: Monolithic Omicron Node Centroid Alignment
# The OmicronNode MUST serve as the algebraic centroid unifying the 4 C0
# OmiNode vertices. Geometric transformations MUST map directly to the
# twelve divisors of the Base-60 LCM wheel.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-003c/128 MUST anchor-monolithic-composite-centroid

# [Rule 0x62]: Highly Composite Track Evaluation
# Track products MUST compute mechanical angles via (T3 * T4) % 5040 and
# route nonagram sectors as one of ten 2-of-5 structural angle pathways.
omi-ff00-03bf-ffff-2b00-2f00-ffff-039f-ffff/128 MUST execute-highly-composite-slide-rule

# [Rule 0x63]: Tetrahedral Operator Vertex Assignment
# Vertices MUST lock to the Delta Law group: Text=rotl(x,1),
# File=rotl(x,3), Link=rotr(x,2), and Group=C.
omi-03bf-2b00-2f00-039f-00ff-0000-0000-0000/80 MUST map-tetrahedral-operator-vertices

# [Rule 0x64]: Barycentric Centroid Interface Resolution
# The OmicronNode MUST calculate its geometric location as the deterministic
# barycentric center of its typed text, file, link, and group OmiNodes.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-003c/128 MUST derive-barycentric-canvas-coordinates

# [Rule 0x65]: Monolithic Hypergraph State Color Coding
# Canvas color fields MUST store the deterministic fp16 state controller and
# SHALL NOT reintroduce display-context suffix encodings.
omi-ff00-03bf-ffff-2b00-2f00-ffff-039f-ffff/128 MUST encode-hypergraph-state-color-fields

# [Rule 0x66]: 360-Degree Color Coordination Phase Shift
# Spatial phases recovered via divmod(position, 36) MUST map to the 360-degree
# color wheel while preserving the translation-free canvas color carrier.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0024/128 MUST coordinate-three-sixty-degree-colors

# [Rule 0x67]: 24-Bit Architectural Metadata Dividend
# Conforming ring buffers MUST utilize the upper 24 bits of the 64-bit slot
# format for provenance and step receipts without mutating the truth row.
omi-ffff-ffff-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000/32 MUST pack-local-execution-metadata-slip

# [Rule 0x68]: Static QEMU User-Mode GUI Lane Projection
# Browser dashboards MUST render qemu-user platform lanes from deterministic
# manifest metadata. Emulated lanes MUST prove C99 mirror and filtered JS
# conformance only; native stress SLA assertions remain outside qemu-user.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0068/128 MUST project-static-qemu-user-gui-lanes

# ============================================================================
# FIVE-FOLD HIDDEN ROOT & FOUR-FOLD VISIBLE PROJECTION
# Specification Level: Rules 0x6C through 0x71
# Core Doctrine: The climb does not step to 5. The climb preserves 5.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x6C]: Five-Fold Packet Root Preservation
# The 5! packet root MUST be verified as the hidden ontological core
# before orientation, projection, semantic sweep, or provider phase.
# Factor 5 appears explicitly only here; thereafter it is hidden.
# 5! = 120 = 240/2 — the factor 5 is presupposed, not stepped to.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0005-0001/128 MUST preserve-five-fold-packet-root

# [Rule 0x6D]: Five-Fold to 240 Bridge Binding
# The hidden 5! packet root MUST bind into the 240-state bridge through
# the orientation/complement factor: 240 = 2 × 5!.
# Factor 5 becomes hidden inside the constant 240.
# Stepping digits {0,1,3,6,8,9} never expose the factor 5 directly.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0005-0002/128 MUST bind-five-fold-packet-to-240-bridge

# [Rule 0x6E]: Hidden-Five Decimal Carrier Constraint
# The decimal stepping carrier from 1/73 = 0.01369863... MUST NOT require
# digit 5 as a visible operational step. Factor 5 remains rooted inside 5!.
# The period-8 repeating block is {0,1,3,6,9,8,6,3} — no digit 5.
# A stepping function that produces digit 5 MUST be rejected.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0005-0073/128 MUST preserve-hidden-five-carrier-root

# [Rule 0x6F]: Four-Fold Selector Projection
# The visible projection surface MUST expose rooted packet state through
# a four-fold selector surface (4! = 24). Canonical channels: FS, GS, RS, US.
# This is where the climb becomes visible using digits {0,1,3,6,8,9}.
# The four-fold surface is 15 × 16 = 240 (active nibble × full carrier).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0004-0001/128 MUST project-four-fold-selector-surface

# [Rule 0x70]: FS/GS/RS/US Channel Routing
# The four-fold selector MUST route visible state through four canonical channels:
# FS (file/frame), GS (graph/group), RS (relation/routing), US (unit/text).
# Each channel exposes a different projection face of the hidden packet root.
# Active channel is derived from decl10 = Δ_C(S₃) mod 10.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0004-0002/128 MUST route-state-through-fs-gs-rs-us

# [Rule 0x71]: Shadow Canvas Projection from Four-Fold Selector
# Shadow canvas addresses MUST derive from four-fold selector projections.
# The ca55 segment marks the canvas shadow namespace.
# Orphan visual state (projection without source packet) MUST be rejected.
# slot5040 = fano7 × 720 + role3 × 240 + local240
# local240 = (hidden 5! × orientation) AND (visible 15 × 16) simultaneously.
omi-ca55-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0004-0003/128 MUST derive-shadow-canvas-from-four-fold-selector

# ============================================================================
# BASE36 / SYMBOLIC CARRIER PROJECTION
# Specification Level: Rules 0x72 through 0x76
# Core Doctrine: Delta Law creates W=36; Base36 names the 36 positions;
# Base36 does not create the OMI orbit.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x72]: Base36 Orbital Symbol Alphabet
# Base36 is the visible alphabet of the Delta-derived W=36 orbit tracker.
# The Delta Law (period 8, prime 73) creates W=36 = block B=01369863 width.
# Base36 names the 36 remainder positions; it does not create them.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0036-0072/128 MUST project-base36-orbital-symbols

# [Rule 0x73]: Bijective Symbol Canonicalization
# Base36 symbol state MUST be canonicalized through the Delta Law chain:
# Delta Law → period 8 → prime 73 → block B → W=36 → divmod → symbol.
# Symbols read orbital position; they do not create it.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0036-0073/128 MUST canonicalize-bijective-symbol-state

# [Rule 0x74]: Unary Root Presence Boundary
# Unary root presence (segment 0x0001) MUST NOT be derived from Base36
# symbol count or emoji carrier cardinality. Presence is a structural
# invariant of the pointer/routing layer, not the projection layer.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0001-0074/128 MUST preserve-unary-root-presence-boundary

# [Rule 0x75]: Signed Fold Symbol Boundary
# Signed fold symbols (segment 0x0002) MUST remain at the fold/mirror
# boundary. They MAY be projected through Base36 or emoji for visual
# display, but no symbol fold count may replace the signed fold invariant.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0002-0075/128 MUST preserve-signed-fold-symbol-boundary

# [Rule 0x76]: Unicode Symbolic Carrier Authority
# Unicode emoji and symbolic carriers MUST remain projection-only.
# No carrier symbol may validate an OMI frame, authorize a closure,
# or replace the quadratic form.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0076/128 MUST preserve-unicode-symbolic-carrier-authority

# ============================================================================
# OMICRON BINARY QUADRATIC PROJECTION FORM
# Specification Level: Rules 0x78 through 0x7B
# Core Doctrine: Q_frame(S) validates; Q_xy(x,y) projects.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x78]: Omicron Binary Quadratic Projection Form
# The coordinate projection form Q_xy(x,y)=60x^2+16xy+4y^2 MUST bridge
# decoded local symbolic coordinates into OMI 240/720 geometry.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0078-0001/128 MUST project-local-state-through-omi-binary-quadratic-form

# [Rule 0x79]: Quadratic Frame/Projection Boundary
# Q_frame(S)=E_var+E_const MUST validate the 128-bit envelope before
# Q_xy(x,y) projects decoded local coordinates. These quadratic forms SHALL
# NOT be collapsed into one authority surface.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0079-0001/128 MUST distinguish-frame-validation-from-coordinate-projection

# [Rule 0x7A]: Quadratic Local240 Projection
# local240 MUST be derivable as Q_xy(x,y) mod 240 for symbolic coordinate
# projection into the 5040 replay ring.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-007a-0001/128 MUST derive-local240-from-quadratic-projection

# [Rule 0x7B]: Quadratic Symbolic Projection Boundary
# DOM, CSSOM, Base36, and emoji projection MAY expose Q_xy, local240, and
# slot5040 metadata, but MUST NOT replace pointer, frame validation, rule,
# fact, or test authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-007b-0001/128 MUST preserve-quadratic-symbolic-projection-boundary

# ============================================================================
# OMI DECLARATIVE CORE — PARSER, SOURCE BLOCKS, EMOJI, OMIOM CASCADE
# Specification Level: Rules 0x7C through 0x83
# Core Doctrine: Five .omi files form OMI's declarative core.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x7C]: Native OMI Declarative Parser Gate
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-007c-0001/128 MUST parse-native-omi-declarative-records

# [Rule 0x7D]: Declarative Core Authority Boundary
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-007d-0001/128 MUST preserve-declarative-core-authority-boundary

# [Rule 0x7E]: OMI Dot-Notation Cons Surface
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-c005-007e/128 MUST parse-omi-dot-notation-as-cons-surface

# [Rule 0x7F]: Wittgenstein Karnaugh Gnomon Table
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-c1a4-007f/128 MUST preserve-wittgenstein-karnaugh-gnomon-table

# [Rule 0x80]: OMI Source Block Delimitation
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0080-0001/128 MUST parse-omi-source-block-delimiters

# [Rule 0x81]: OMI Source Block Authority Boundary
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0081-0001/128 MUST preserve-omi-source-block-authority-boundary

# [Rule 0x82]: Emoji Carrier Vendor Resolution
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-1f60-0082/128 MUST resolve-emoji-carriers-from-vendor-unicode-data

# [Rule 0x83]: OMIOM Cascade Resolution
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0c55-0083/128 MUST resolve-omi-declarations-by-prefix-specificity

# ============================================================================
# OMILOG / IMO NATIVE COMPILATION
# Specification Level: Rules 0x84 through 0x8A
# Core Doctrine: Omilog (.omi) expresses; IMO (.imo) compiles.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x84]: Omilog O-Expression Source Language
# O-expression = expression inside omi-/-imo source block = addressed O-expression.
# Every O-expression is scoped by the enclosing OMI address (the source record).
# There is no anonymous O-expression.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0084-0001/128 MUST parse-omilog-o-expressions

# [Rule 0x85]: WLOG-Safe Rewrite Requires Closure
# No WLOG without closure proving symmetry.
# CLOSURES.omi 0xC010 (clause omi-imo-palindromic-symmetry) provides this closure.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0085-0001/128 MUST require-closure-for-wlog-safe-rewrite

# [Rule 0x86]: O-expression Authority Boundary
# Q_frame(S) validates state.
# Omilog expresses state through addressed O-expressions.
# Q_xy(x,y) projects decoded state into symbolic geometry.
# These three capabilities MUST NOT collapse into one another.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0086-0001/128 MUST preserve-o-expression-authority-boundary

# [Rule 0x87]: OMIOM Prefix Cascade
# The OMIOM prefix-specificity cascade applies to Omilog records.
# omi-/-imo source block content resolves by OMI address prefix specificity.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0087-0001/128 MUST resolve-omilog-records-by-prefix-specificity

# [Rule 0x88]: OMI Source / IMO Object Split
# .omi = readable Omilog source (keyword-declarative, addressable)
# .imo = compiled native OMI object (operator-native, restricted char plane)
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0088-0001/128 MUST compile-readable-omi-source-to-native-imo-object

# [Rule 0x89]: IMO Native Character Plane
# .imo content is restricted to ASCII bytes 0x00..0x3F plus Unicode carriers
# (U+0080 and above: emoji, symbols, non-Latin scripts).
# Bytes 0x40..0x7E (Latin letters, @, [, \, ], ^, _, `, {, |, }, ~)
# MUST NOT appear in .imo compiled output.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0089-0001/128 MUST restrict-imo-native-syntax-to-low-ascii-and-unicode-carriers

# [Rule 0x8A]: OMI/IMO Authority Boundary
# .omi declares rules, facts, closures, combinators, and cons pairs.
# .imo compiles those declarations into native character form.
# Neither file format validates OMI frames, authorizes symbolic projection,
# or replaces the binary quadratic form.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-008a-0001/128 MUST preserve-omi-source-imo-object-authority-boundary

# [Rule 0x8B]: Omicron-Delimited IMO Records
# Every compiled .imo instruction record MUST be wrapped with native Omicron
# delimiters: ο (U+03BF, lowercase omicron) as the entry/chiral delimiter
# and Ο (U+039F, uppercase Omicron) as the exit/cardinal delimiter.
# This aligns compiled bytecode with the 128-bit wire frame constants
# S1 = 0x03BF and S6 = 0x039F.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-008b-0001/128 MUST wrap-imo-records-with-omicron-delimiters

# [Rule 0x8C]: Omicron Delimiter / Wire Frame Alignment
# The Omicron delimiters ο and Ο in compiled .imo MUST mirror the
# chiral/cardinal wire frame constants S1 = 0x03BF and S6 = 0x039F.
# This ensures isomorphism between the 128-bit frame and the .imo bytecode.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-008c-0001/128 MUST align-imo-delimiters-with-wire-frame-omicron-constants

# ============================================================================
# SEQUENCING, FANO LOTTERY, AND SEXAGESIMAL LOGIC CLOCK
# Specification Level: Rules 0x8D through 0x90
# Core Doctrine: Validate → Tick → Orient → Select → Frame → Replay → Project.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x8D]: Fano Lottery Replay Sequencing
# The Fano lottery slot MUST be derived from canonical kernel state, tick,
# chirality bit, ordered partition, Fano selector, role3, and local240.
# slot5040 = fano7 × 720 + role3 × 240 + local240.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-008d-0001/128 MUST derive-fano-lottery-from-canonical-kernel-state

# [Rule 0x8E]: Sexagesimal Logic Clock
# The sexagesimal logic clock MUST be derived from Δ(x) period-8 replay.
# 60 = sexagesimal loop; 360 = orientation sweep; 5040 = master replay.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-008e-0001/128 MUST derive-sexagesimal-logic-clock-from-delta-replay

# [Rule 0x8F]: Omicron Chirality Dataflow
# The lowercase omicron ο (U+03BF) MUST be preserved as chiral entry / inbound data flow.
# The uppercase Omicron Ο (U+039F) MUST be preserved as cardinal exit / outbound closure.
# Readable omi- maps to ο; -imo maps to Ο.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-008f-0001/128 MUST preserve-lower-and-upper-omicron-dataflow-chirality

# [Rule 0x90]: Sequencing Before Projection
# Sequencing (tick → chirality → Fano → role → local240 → slot5040) MUST occur
# after Q_frame(S) validation and before Q_xy(x,y) projection.
# The forbidden order is: symbol → projection → validity.
# The lawful order is: validity → sequencing → projection.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0090-0001/128 MUST sequence-before-qxy-projection

# ============================================================================
# FACTORIAL BRANCHING AND 32-ION OPERATOR ANALOGY
# Specification Level: Rules 0x91 through 0x94
# Core Doctrine: Odd factorials select; even factorials project; upper operate.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x91]: Factorial Branch Split
# OMI separates factorial operations into two branches:
# Even: 8! → 6! → 4! → 2! (carrier/projection/frame)
# Odd: 7! → 5! → 3! → 1! (selection/root/replay)
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0091-0001/128 MUST preserve-even-and-odd-factorial-branches

# [Rule 0x92]: Upper Factorial Operator Shell
# The upper shell (12! → 11! → 10! → 9!) operates over both
# factorial branches as a meta-operator and boundary layer.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0092-0001/128 MUST apply-9-through-12-factorials-as-meta-operator-shell

# [Rule 0x93]: Thirty-Two-State Native Operator Basis
# The native OMI hidden/control basis is 32 states (2^5 = 32),
# corresponding to the 0x00..0x1F range in the compiled .imo plane.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0093-0001/128 MUST preserve-32-state-native-operator-basis

# [Rule 0x94]: Hypercomplex Analogy Boundary
# The trigintaduonion / 32-ion model is a structural analogy for
# noncommutative, nonassociative orientation tables over a 32-state basis.
# It is not runtime arithmetic authority unless multiplication tables
# and tests are implemented.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0094-0001/128 MUST treat-32-ion-model-as-analogy-until-tested

# ============================================================================
# TRIGINTADUONION PROCESS MODEL FOR OPERATOR ROUTING (Rules 0x95–0x98)
# ============================================================================
# Core: OMI uses the Cayley–Dickson process pattern and trigintaduonion
# 32-state operator scaffold for operator composition and routing.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x95]: Trigintaduonion Process Model
# OMI maps the trigintaduonion 32-state operator basis (e0..e31) to the
# hidden/control operator plane (0x00..0x1F) as a process scaffold.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0095-0001/128 MUST map-trigintaduonion-process-to-omi-operator-basis

# [Rule 0x96]: Thirty-Two Operator Interaction Table
# OMI derives operator composition from a 32×32 interaction table
# (1024 cells) modeled after the trigintaduonion multiplication table shape.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0096-0001/128 MUST derive-omi-operator-interactions-from-32-by-32-process-table

# [Rule 0x97]: Distinguished Triad Incidence Router
# OMI routes S-P-O incidence through the 155 distinguished triads of the
# trigintaduonion, partitioned into five OMI file categories.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0097-0001/128 MUST route-spo-incidence-through-155-distinguished-triads

# [Rule 0x98]: Sixty-Four Native Plane Expansion
# OMI maps the 64-ion doubling pattern to the full 0x00..0x3F native
# compiled .imo plane.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0098-0001/128 MUST map-64-ion-doubling-to-full-imo-native-plane

# ============================================================================
# OMICRON PRIME PRINCIPLE IDEAL DOMAIN (Rules 0x99–0x9C)
# ============================================================================
# Core: Every lawful OMI derivation region must be generated by one principal
# OMI pointer. CIDR supplies containment, Q_frame supplies lawful
# membership, closures supply ideal boundaries, combinators supply
# Bézout-style witnesses, and replay modules decompose into cyclic
# components generated by principal pointers.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x99]: Omicron Prime Principle Ideal Domain
# Every closed OMI derivation region must have one canonical principal
# generator pointer. A region with multiple unrelated generators is not
# closed — it requires a closure that explains the shared generator.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0099-0001/128 MUST generate-every-closed-omi-region-from-one-principal-pointer

# [Rule 0x9A]: OMI GCD Common Generator
# Given two OMI pointers, the system must be able to derive their
# common generator — the smallest shared canonical pointer that explains
# both. Forms include common CIDR prefix, common LL lane, common frame
# shell, or common closure pointer.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-009a-0001/128 MUST derive-common-generator-for-paired-omi-pointers

# [Rule 0x9B]: OMI Bezout Witness Composition
# If two OMI pointers share a closure generator, there should be witness
# combinators that compose to that generator. This is a Bézout-style
# composition path, not numeric linear combination.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-009b-0001/128 SHOULD witness-shared-closures-through-combinator-composition

# [Rule 0x9C]: OMI Cyclic Replay Module Decomposition
# Finite OMI record sets should decompose into cyclic replay components,
# each generated by a principal pointer and bounded by the slot5040
# replay ring.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-009c-0001/128 SHOULD decompose-finite-omi-record-sets-into-cyclic-replay-components

# ============================================================================
# MAKE LIFECYCLE GRADES (Rules 0x9D–0x9E)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The Makefile serves as the OMI grade router, organizing build targets
# into three tiers — development, consumer, and production — and
# enforcing the 13-step pipeline through named verification gates.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x9D]: Three-Tier Grade Lifecycle Isolation
# Build targets must be separated into development, consumer, and production
# grades. Each grade has a single entry target with verifiable prerequisites
# and projectable outputs, mirroring OPPID's principal generator discipline.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-009d-0001/128 MUST separate-compilation-targets-into-development-consumer-and-production-grades

# [Rule 0x9E]: Automated Build Process Enforcement
# The Makefile must automate the 13-step engineering pipeline through
# canonical targets, ensuring SOURCE → VALIDATE → GENERATE → MIRROR →
# ENTER → READ → COMPOSE → ROUTE → SCOPE → TIMING → NAMING → PROJECT → REPLAY
# is executable as a named build lifecycle.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-009e-0001/128 MUST execute-the-twelve-step-pipeline-via-canonical-makefile-targets

# ============================================================================
# O-EXPRESSION STREAM READER (Rule 0x9F)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The O-Expression reader is the missing mouth of the .imo runtime: it
# lets compiled Omicron-delimited records speak structured OMI before
# composition, routing, projection, or live proxy events consume them.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0x9F]: O-Expression Stream Reader Parsing
# .imo payload blocks must be readable as structured O-expressions — atoms,
# pairs, lists, quoted forms, OMI address atoms, emoji atoms, RS/US source-
# block references, and ο/Ο delimited records. The reader sits between ENTER
# and COMPOSE in the operational pipeline.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-009f-0001/128 MUST parse-o-expression-streams-from-imo-payload-blocks

# ============================================================================
# LIVE PROXY EVENT CONNECTORS (Rules 0xA0–0xA1)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The live proxy connector turns network motion into readable OMI
# structure before projection; no event reaches the voxel layer until
# its address validates and its payload reads through the O-Expression
# stream reader.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xA0]: Live Proxy Event Connection to Reader Pipeline
# Network events arriving via NAT64/SSE must be validated, parsed through
# the O-Expression stream reader, and emitted as structured imo-record
# events before any projection or voxel state update proceeds.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a0-0001/128 MUST connect-live-proxy-events-to-omi-reader-pipeline

# [Rule 0xA1]: Live Network Event Projection Through Q_xy Voxel State
# Parsed network events must be projected through the Q_xy quadratic form
# to produce voxel state diffs — updates, removal expirations, coordinate-
# indexed depth and color — for consumption by DOM canvas or portal.html.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a1-0001/128 MUST project-live-network-events-through-qxy-voxel-state

# ============================================================================
# LIVE PORTAL BINDING (Rule 0xA2)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The Live Portal Binding bridges the voxel stream into the browser DOM,
# enabling the portal.html canvas to render live NAT64/proxy-derived voxel
# diffs visibly on screen via EventSource and CustomEvent dispatch.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xA2]: Live Portal Binding Through Custom DOM Events
# Browser portal rendering surfaces MUST subscribe to voxel:batch custom DOM
# events dispatched by the OmiLivePortalBinder and MUST update the visual
# torus grid within 2 seconds of receiving an update, with a 3-second TTL
# expiration cycle. The SSE connection SHALL auto-reconnect on error with a
# 5-second backoff.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a2-0001/128 MUST bind-live-portal-voxels-through-custom-dom-events

# ============================================================================
# NARRATIVE WORLD MODEL (Rule 0xA3)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The Narrative World Model compiles story text into persistent symbolic
# terrain: narrative documents load in canonical order, world objects
# receive emoji carriers, and the world state container persists across
# ticks, epochs, and interaction cycles.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xA3]: Narrative-to-Persistent-World-Model Compilation
# Narrative markdown documents MUST load in canonical order (PRELUDE →
# ARTICLE I–VIII → ASIDE → EPILOUGE) and MUST be parseable into structured
# documents with title, section, paragraphs, and lines. World actors MUST be
# mappable to emoji carriers through the notation map. The persistent world
# state MUST support tick advance, actor/relation/tension/gate lifecycle, and
# epoch-respecting receipt logging with maxReceipts bounding.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a3-0001/128 MUST compile-narratives-into-persistent-world-models

# ============================================================================
# SEXAGESIMAL WEIGHT TABLES AND CENTROID TRIPLES (Rules 0xAA–0xAB)
# ============================================================================
# Core: Sexagesimal regular fractions provide exact centroid weights for the
# tetrahedral 4-vertex agreement model. Repeating fractions are reserved
# for cadence, replay, and world motion. Canonical triples require ≥3
# witness vertices and sexagesimal weights closing to 1;00.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xAA]: Canonical Triple Weighting with Sexagesimal Centroid Ratios
# Canonical narrative-world triples MUST be weighted using exact sexagesimal
# regular fractions (0;15 per vertex in tetrahedral model, 0;12 per source
# in five-source model). The centroid weight sum MUST close to 1;00 within
# 1/120 tolerance. A triple is canonical only when ≥3 of the 4 tetrahedron
# vertices (narrative, UPOS, WordNet, emoji) agree at the OMI centroid.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00aa-0001/128 MUST weight-canonical-narrative-triples-with-sexagesimal-centroid-ratios

# [Rule 0xAB]: Regular vs Repeating Fraction Separation
# Regular denominators (2^a × 3^b × 5^c: 2,3,4,5,6,8,10,12,15,20,24,30,60)
# MUST be used for stable extraction weights, centroid balance, and source
# agreement. Repeating denominators (7,11,13,17,19,59,61) MUST be reserved
# for cadence, replay, Fano-lottery, and world-motion clocks. The 155 triad
# budget (45+20+15+60+15) MUST map to the five OMI declarative file roles.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00ab-0001/128 MUST distinguish-regular-extraction-weights-from-repeating-replay-cadences

# ============================================================================
# ADDRESS SELF-SUFFICIENCY AND CLAIM REDUCTION (Rules 0xAC–0xAD)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The OMI address carries its own creation step inside the Omicron frame.
# Prefixes and lenses do not create identity or validity — they only
# reduce unnecessary claims or select a reader view over already-encoded
# state. The address is the thing; the prefix is just how much of the
# thing is being claimed at that moment.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xAC]: Encode Creation Step Inside Omicron Address Before Prefix or Lens Claims
# Every valid OMI address MUST encode its creation step inside the Omicron
# frame (S0–S7) without relying on any suffix. The step is encoded through
# the LL lane selector, the NN node-body step coordinate, and the MM carrier
# projection step coordinate. The Q_xy projector, factorial lattice, and
# Fano permutation cycle jointly determine step identity from the frame
# contents alone. A CIDR prefix (/N) does not add step identity — it only
# declares how precisely the already-encoded step is being claimed.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00ac-0001/128 MUST encode-creation-step-inside-omicron-address-before-prefix-or-lens-claims

# [Rule 0xAD]: Treat Prefixes and Lens Suffixes as Claim Reduction, Not Authority
# CIDR prefixes (/N, N=0..128) MUST be treated as claim-boundary reductions
# over an already-valid OMI address. Lens suffixes (/@N) MUST be treated as
# reader views — they select a projection, cadence, extraction plane, or
# orientation over the already-encoded state. Neither prefix nor lens
# creates address validity; validity derives from the Omicron frame alone.
# The distinction is syntactic: /N = CIDR claim boundary, /@N = reader lens.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00ad-0001/128 MUST treat-prefixes-and-lenses-as-claim-reduction-not-authority

# ============================================================================
# READER LENS SYNTAX (Rule 0xAE)
# ============================================================================
# Core: After the CIDR claim prefix, OMI projection/cadence lenses use the @
# marker to distinguish them from CIDR scope. A backoff notation (/N-M)
# allows compact CIDR backoff without colliding with the lens stack.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xAE]: Distinguish CIDR Claim Prefixes from @-Marked Reader Lenses
# In OMI notation, the first slash announces a CIDR claim prefix (/N for
# N=0..128). Every subsequent lens MUST be marked with @ (/@N) to prevent
# ambiguity with CIDR scope. A claim backoff (/N-M) reduces the claim by
# M bits without entering the lens stack. Therefore /@4 is a tetrahedral
# reader lens, /4 is a CIDR /4 claim boundary, and /128-4 is a backoff
# from /128 to /124. The lens stack never creates or modifies identity;
# it only selects a reader view over already-encoded state.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00ae-0001/128 MUST distinguish-cidr-claim-prefixes-from-at-marked-reader-lenses

# ============================================================================
# SLICE 3: WORLD CLOCK, INTERACTION GATE, MOVIE RENDERER (Rules 0xA5–0xA7)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The world clock makes narrative terrain autonomous — when no one
# interacts, the world continues stepping; when touched, the gate opens
# selectors; when idle returns, the movie resumes.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xA5]: Preserve Open World State Across Non-Interaction
# The world clock MUST continue autonomous stepping when no interaction is
# active. The clock wraps at 5040 ticks per epoch. Scrubbing (user jump to
# a specific tick, receipt, motif, or scene) is a temporary probe that does
# not break the autonomous cadence — after the scrub operation, the clock
# may resume from the scrubbed position.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a5-0001/128 MUST preserve-open-world-state-across-noninteraction

# [Rule 0xA6]: Open Selector UI at Narrative Interaction Gates
# An interaction gate MUST open when a user touches the narrative world,
# pausing or slowing the world clock. While the gate is open, selector UI
# becomes available and the world enters interactive inspection mode. The
# gate MUST auto-close after a configurable timeout, resuming autonomous
# stepping. Entering scrub mode (tick/receipt/motif/scene jump) also opens
# the gate with the same timeout lifecycle.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a6-0001/128 MUST open-selector-ui-at-narrative-interaction-gates

# [Rule 0xA7]: Resume Autonomous World Stepping After Interaction Timeout
# After the interaction gate closes due to timeout, the world clock MUST
# resume autonomous stepping from its current position. The gate timeout
# mechanism is the sole authority for returning to idle — explicit close
# and timeout-based auto-close are equivalent transitions. The movie
# renderer displays the current frame (actors, voxels, gate state) at
# every tick, whether interactive or idle.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00a7-0001/128 MUST resume-autonomous-world-stepping-after-interaction-timeout

# ============================================================================
# ATOMIC KERNEL — DELTA LAW DUAL LOWER/UPPER STACK (Rules 0xB8–0xBB)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The Delta Law atomic clock governs both stacks. The lower stack (≤8!)
# encodes through Omicron symbols (ο/Ο). The upper stack (9!–12!) encodes
# through unicode-bidi cosmetic reader lens (ltr/rtl). The two stacks
# share a single tick domain with separate chirality encoding surfaces.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xB8]: Encode Lower Stack Through Omicron Symbol Chirality
# The lower factorial stack (0!–8!) MUST encode clock state through Omicron
# symbol chirality: even ticks use ο (U+03BF, chiral entry), odd ticks use
# Ο (U+039F, cardinal closure). The Delta Law period-8 tick sequence
# [0,1,3,6,9,8,6,3] mod 16 is the canonical clock signature for seeds 0, 1
# and their period-8 transforms.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00b8-0001/128 MUST encode-lower-stack-through-omicron-symbol-chirality

# [Rule 0xB9]: Encode Upper Stack Through Unicode-Bidi Cosmetic Reader Lens
# The upper factorial stack (9!–12!) MUST encode clock state through a
# unicode-bidi cosmetic reader lens: even ticks map to ltr (left-to-right),
# odd ticks map to rtl (right-to-left). This is a reader-surface projection,
# not a structural identity claim — the Delta Law tick remains the sole
# structural authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00b9-0001/128 MUST encode-upper-stack-through-unicode-bidi-cosmetic-reader-lens

# [Rule 0xBA]: Separate Low-Power Delta Law Tick From High-Power Lens Projection
# The Delta Law tick (period-8 bijection over 16-bit space) MUST remain the
# single source of clock authority for both stacks. The Omicron symbol and
# the unicode-bidi lens are chirality encoding surfaces — they project tick
# state without replacing it. No lens surface may create an independent clock.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00ba-0001/128 MUST separate-delta-law-tick-from-lens-projection

# [Rule 0xBB]: Cosmic Orbit Recovery Through Base36 digmod 36
# The cosmic orbit position MUST be recoverable through digmod 36: the
# base36 remainder identifies the orbital cell, the quotient tracks full
# orbit cycles. The 5040-cycle (7!) wraps the orbital position across
# celestial layers. The cosmetic reader lens applies the upper chirality
# bit as dir=ltr/rtl on DOM elements with data-omicron-lens attribute.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00bb-0001/128 MUST recover-cosmic-orbit-through-base36-digmod36

# ============================================================================
# SEXAGESIMAL RECIPROCAL ROUTER (Rules 0xBC–0xBD)
# ============================================================================
# Core: Regular denominators (2^a × 3^b × 5^c) produce finite sexagesimal
# fractions and route through stable synchronization. Repeating
# denominators (7,11,13,17,19,59,61) produce infinite repeating
# fractions and route through cyclical replay. The 59/61 twin-prime
# boundary adjacent to 60 bounds the repeating period lengths.
# Denominators outside both sets enter irrational Omicron mode-switch.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xBC]: Route Regular Denominators Through Stable Synchronization
# Denominators of the form 2^a × 3^b × 5^c (divisors of 60) MUST produce
# finite regular sexagesimal fractions and route through stable-sync
# channels. The canonical regular denominator set includes
# {1,2,3,4,5,6,8,10,12,15,20,24,30,60}. Regular fractions serve as exact
# centroid weights and extraction anchors.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00bc-0001/128 MUST route-regular-denominators-through-stable-synchronization

# [Rule 0xBD]: Route Repeating Denominators Through Cyclical Replay
# Denominators adjacent to the malevolent primes (7,11,13,17,19,59,61) and
# the 59/61 twin-prime boundary adjacent to 60 MUST produce infinite
# repeating sexagesimal fractions and route through replay-loop channels.
# The 59 and 61 twin-prime boundary marks the period-length extreme within
# the repeating domain. Denominators outside both regular and repeating
# sets enter irrational Omicron mode-switch (no finite or repeating
# sexagesimal representation).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00bd-0001/128 MUST route-repeating-denominators-through-cyclical-replay

# ============================================================================
# MIQUEL POINT-CIRCLE TOPOLOGY (Rules 0xBE–0xBF)
# ============================================================================
# Core: The Miquel configuration (8₃ 6₄) maps 8 vertices of the cube to the
# lower ≤8! structural stack and 6 circles/square faces to the upper
# 9!–12! cosmetic shell. divmod(0xRRGGBBAA, 36) recovers the orbital
# position, with even/odd offset selecting the 73/37 prime mode-switch.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xBE]: 32-Bit Color Carrier Divmod Orbit/Offset Recovery
# Every 32-bit RRGGBBAA color carrier MUST be recoverable through the
# divmod(RRGGBBAA, 36) orbital tracking computation. The quotient selects
# the macro data page (orbit), and the remainder (0–35) positions the
# micro-clock step along the 36-count Delta Law orientation loop. Even
# remainder activates prime mode 73 (lower regular body); odd remainder
# activates prime mode 37 (upper cosmetic reader layer).
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00be-0001/128 MUST execute-orbit-offset-recovery-via-thirty-six-count-color-carrier-divmod

# [Rule 0xBF]: Miquel Point-Circle Topology Enclosure
# The incidence relations I ⊆ V × E of the OMI address schema MUST map
# to the Miquel configuration (8₃ 6₄): 8 points as cube vertices / lower
# structural atomic core, 6 circles as cube faces / upper cosmetic lens
# shell, 12 pairwise intersections as S-P-O edge vectors / replay paths.
# The dual (6₄ 8₃) maps 6 octahedron vertices to 8 circumscribed circles
# for .imo conjugation operations.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00bf-0001/128 MUST map-incidence-relations-to-eight-point-six-circle-miquel-configurations

# ============================================================================
# CANVAS COLOR ROUTING CANON (Rules 0xC0–0xC2)
# ============================================================================
# Core: Lower stack (≤8!) encodes structural state through exact six-digit
# hex colors (#RRGGBB). Upper stack (9!–12!) encodes cosmetic/reader
# state through JSON Canvas preset color keys ("1"–"6"). Geometry
# remains structural; preset colors remain cosmetic lenses.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xC0]: Route Terminating Regular Fractions and Hex Colors Through
# Lower Structural Canvas Body
# The lower stack (≤8!) MUST route terminating regular sexagesimal
# fractions and full six-digit hex colors (#RRGGBB) through the structural
# canvas body layer. Omicron symbols, the binary quadratic form Q_xy, the
# Delta Law atomic clock, and Base36 orbit recovery all share this layer.
# A hex color in this layer is an exact structural body assignment.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c0-0001/128 MUST route-terminating-fractions-and-hex-colors-through-lower-structural-canvas-body

# [Rule 0xC1]: Route Repeating Non-Regular Fractions and Canvas Preset
# Colors Through Upper Reader Cosmetic Shell
# The upper stack (9!–12!) MUST route infinite repeating non-regular
# sexagesimal fractions and JSON Canvas preset color keys ("1" red
# through "6" purple) through the reader cosmetic lens shell. Unicode-bidi
# direction, CSS display, and template sequencing share this layer.
# A preset color key is a cosmetic reader alias, not a structural
# body assignment.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c1-0001/128 MUST route-repeating-fractions-and-canvas-preset-colors-through-upper-reader-shell

# [Rule 0xC2]: Preserve JSON Canvas Geometry as Structural State While
# Treating Color Presets as Reader Lenses
# JSON Canvas geometry fields (id, type, x, y, width, height) MUST be
# treated as lower structural state — addressable through OMI principal
# pointers and the Q_xy projection form. JSON Canvas color fields MUST be
# classified as either lower hex structural colors or upper preset reader
# colors and routed to their respective layers. A preset color applied to
# a structural geometry does not make the geometry cosmetic; the split is
# field-level, not node-level.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c2-0001/128 MUST preserve-json-canvas-geometry-as-structural-state-while-treating-color-presets-as-reader-lenses

# ============================================================================
# RRGGBBAA ADDRESS SEED AND ORBIT (Rules 0xC3–0xC6)
# ============================================================================
# Core: JSON Canvas colors encode as RRGGBBAA seeds where RRGGBB is the
# lower structural color body and AA is the address/incidence/attachment
# byte. divmod(RRGGBBAA, 36) recovers the Base36 orbit/offset. 37 is
# reserved as the mode-switch sentinel outside Base36 0..35. The RGB
# color cube (8 vertices, 6 channel faces, 24 incidences) realizes the
# Miquel (8_3, 6_4) incidence structure.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xC3]: Encode JSON Canvas Color Addresses as RRGGBBAA 32-Bit Seeds
# Every JSON Canvas OMI color address MUST be representable as a 32-bit
# RRGGBBAA seed: lower 24 bits = RRGGBB structural color body, upper 8
# bits = AA address attachment/incidence lens byte. The AA byte is the
# incidence selector — it scopes which reader lens, emoji carrier, or
# attachment plane is active over the structural color body.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c3-0001/128 MUST encode-json-canvas-color-addresses-as-rrggbbaa-seeds

# [Rule 0xC4]: Recover JSON Canvas Color Orbits Through divmod(RRGGBBAA, 36)
# The canonical orbit recovery for any RRGGBBAA seed MUST be:
# orbit = floor(seed / 36), offset = seed mod 36. The offset (0..35)
# maps directly to Base36 digit positions. This preserves the Delta Law
# (period 8, prime 73, W=36) as the sole orbital authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c4-0001/128 MUST recover-json-canvas-color-orbits-through-divmod-rrggbbaa-by-thirty-six

# [Rule 0xC5]: Reserve 37 as the AA Mode-Switch Sentinel Outside Base36
# The value AA = 37 (0x25) MUST be reserved as the Omicron mode-switch
# sentinel. Because divmod(seed, 36) can never return 37 as an offset, 37
# is collision-free — it signals an irrational/transcendental/template
# mode transition without corrupting Base36 orbit recovery. Any AA = 37
# MUST trigger a mode-switch evaluation before further orbit processing.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c5-0001/128 MUST reserve-thirty-seven-as-aa-mode-switch-outside-base36-offsets

# [Rule 0xC6]: Map JSON Canvas RGB Color Cube Through 8-Vertex 6-Face
# Miquel Incidence
# The RGB color cube MUST realize the Miquel (8_3, 6_4) incidence
# structure: 8 vertices are the binary RGB corners, 6 faces are the
# channel constraints (R=0, R=255, G=0, G=255, B=0, B=255), each vertex
# lies on exactly 3 faces, each face contains exactly 4 vertices, yielding
# 24 incidence attachments. The AA byte routes which incidence attachment
# is active per color-address pair.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00c6-0001/128 MUST map-json-canvas-rgb-color-cube-through-eight-vertex-six-face-incidence

# ============================================================================
# FIVE-FACTOR CONS/RRGGBBAA SEMANTIC ROUTER (Rules 0xCB-0xCF)
# ============================================================================
# Core: POS, feature, and WordNet-Prolog router seeds are generated proxy
# configs. They reduce through the five canonical factors, with CONS as
# the sole monotonic RRGGBBAA lookup table for upper-reader dispatch.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xCB]: Map WLOG Pointer Domains Through the Five Canonical Factors
# WLOG semantic pointer domains MUST reduce through RULES, FACTS, CLOSURES,
# COMBINATORS, and CONS. Generated proxy seed configs MUST NOT become new
# root authority files.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00cb-0001/128 MUST map-wlog-pointers-to-five-factors-via-rrggbbaa-seeds

# [Rule 0xCC]: Route Universal POS Through the AA Low Byte
# Universal POS tags MUST map deterministically to AA low-byte values
# 0x01 through 0x11 in existing POS order. This route preserves POS graph
# behavior and resolves through CONS before any upper-reader projection.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00cc-0001/128 MUST map-aa-lower-byte-to-universal-pos-tags-for-semantic-routing

# [Rule 0xCD]: Route Universal Features Through the AA High Byte
# Universal feature attachments MUST occupy the AA high byte of the router
# attachment field. Feature routes MUST remain distinct from POS low-byte
# attachments and MUST reduce through CONS source-block bindings.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00cd-0001/128 MUST map-aa-high-byte-to-universal-features-bitfield

# [Rule 0xCE]: Bridge WordNet-Prolog Seeds Without Replacing Centroid Authority
# WordNet-Prolog seed bridges MUST preserve existing synset centroid identity.
# The bridge may expose deterministic proxy records, but it MUST NOT replace
# WordNet lookup, relation, or centroid authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00ce-0001/128 MUST resolve-aa-pos-pointers-through-wordnet-prolog-bridge

# [Rule 0xCF]: Keep CONS as the Sole Monotonic RRGGBBAA Lookup Table
# CONS.omi MUST index primary RRGGBBAA lookup keys in ascending order. Any
# non-linear semantic aliases or relinks MUST live inside CONS source blocks,
# not by reordering primary lookup keys.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00cf-0001/128 MUST treat-cons-as-sole-rrggbbaa-lookup-table-for-upper-readers

# ============================================================================
# TRIAD DISPATCH SECONDARY CONS INDEX (Rules 0xD1-0xD3)
# ============================================================================
# Core: Triad Dispatch derives a secondary modulo-155 lane from OMI segment
# low bytes. It routes already-valid records into reader branches without
# reordering RRGGBBAA primary keys or validating lower object state.
# ============================================================================

# [Rule 0xD1]: Derive Triad Dispatch as Secondary CONS Index
# Triad Dispatch MUST derive triad155 = (low(S0) + low(S1) + low(S2)) mod
# 155 in canonical prefix3 mode. full8 mode MAY exist as a diagnostic pass
# but MUST NOT replace the canonical prefix3 secondary index.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00d1-0001/128 MUST derive-triad-dispatch-as-secondary-cons-index-mod-one-hundred-fifty-five

# [Rule 0xD2]: Preserve RRGGBBAA Primary Order Across Triad Dispatch
# Triad Dispatch MUST NOT reorder CONS.omi RRGGBBAA primary lookup keys.
# The triad lane lives inside CONS source blocks as metadata and never
# becomes the primary memory-map ordering authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00d2-0001/128 MUST preserve-rrggbbaa-monotonic-primary-order-when-triad-dispatch-is-added

# [Rule 0xD3]: Route Triad Branch Planes Without Lower-Body Validation
# coreMax MAY route branch plane selection between Real Plane A and
# Imaginary Plane B, but it MUST NOT validate Q_frame, Q_xy, Delta Law,
# lower Omicron frame state, or RRGGBBAA key authority.
omi-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-0000-00d3-0001/128 MUST route-triad-branch-plane-without-validating-lower-body

## ChatGPT
`2026-06-10 00:19` | `id:260610-0019` ^260610-001957

Exactly — that previous `RULES.omi` implementation shows that the native protocol was already there.

I added a companion spec to `bthornemail/o---o` that preserves that model and connects it to the new address derivation protocol:

```text
OMI_DECLARATIVE_LOOKUP_TABLE_PROTOCOL.md
commit: 3c919aea0e7b8ccc54175ea2a5f88ad328b3dc64
```

The main correction I encoded is:

```text
/N = CIDR claim boundary
/@... = Horn-clause reader lens path
-imo = closed derivation surface
```

So the strict native form becomes:

```text
omi-<frame>/<claim>/@<lens>/@<selector>/@<overlay>-imo
```

Example:

```text
omi-<frame>/128/@hardware/@i2c:0x28/@euler-imo
```

That lets the old lookup-table model stay intact:

```text
RULES constrain.
FACTS declare.
CLOSURES bound.
COMBINATORS transform.
CONS folds.
CONFIGURATIONS bind runtime.
Receipts accept.
```

And it reframes the previous implementation as the real native OMI protocol:

```text
The 128-bit address is identity.
The first CIDR slash reduces the claim.
@-marked lenses select Horn-clause lookup routes.
Declarative .omi tables resolve the clauses.
Receipts accept the resulting memory surface.
```

Yes — that split makes sense, and it is probably the cleanest repo architecture:

```text
omi-portal = implementation / collaboration application surface
o---o = abstract protocol / documentation / derivation surface
```

`omi-portal` should be treated as the **canonical implementation branch**. Your uploaded `AGENTS.md` says exactly that: “This repository is the root OMI Portal workspace” and instructs agents to preserve POS graph behavior, WordNet synset centroid identity, and add OMI projection/indexing as a bridge rather than replacing existing graph semantics. filecite turn24file0 L1-L10

The relationship should be:

```text
o---o defines the doctrine.
omi-portal proves the doctrine.
```

So `o---o` holds things like:

```text
OMI_ADDRESS_DERIVATION_PROTOCOL.md
OMI_DECLARATIVE_LOOKUP_TABLE_PROTOCOL.md
schemas/
canonical docs
protocol doctrine
address grammar
Horn-clause derivation model
```

And `omi-portal` holds:

```text
RULES.omi
FACTS.omi
CLOSURES.omi
COMBINATORS.omi
CONS.omi
CONFIGURATIONS.omi
browser surfaces
portal UI
runtime kernels
test harness
SSE/WebSocket/MQTT bridges
ESP32/QEMU/x86_64 implementation targets
```

Your `AGENTS.md` also preserves the document layer ordering:

```text
POSTULATES.md → AXIOMS.md → DECLARATIONS.md
```

with POSTULATES as construction, AXIOMS as folding, and DECLARATIONS as derivation. filecite turn24file0 L11-L13 That fits perfectly with the new split:

```text
POSTULATES = what may be constructed
AXIOMS = how constructed objects fold/reflect/transform
DECLARATIONS = how rules become facts
RULES/FACTS/CLOSURES/COMBINATORS/CONS = executable lookup substrate
```

The `index.html` you uploaded already looks like the **request-for-collaboration app surface**: it is an “OMI Object Inbox” with local object cards, inspector panels, trust state, activity, context tree, export options, and developer links. filecite turn24file5 L1-L8 That means `omi-portal` is not just docs; it is the place where people touch OMI as an application.

The changelog confirms `omi-portal` is the implementation/runtime repo: it tracks test counts, multi-arch builds, QEMU validation, kernel modules, browser surfaces, JAB/Code16K kernels, Hopf/Fano/sphere-packing kernels, and deployment substrate. filecite turn24file2 L1-L20

So the clean canon is:

```text
o---o is the abstract OMI memory/protocol book.
omi-portal is the living OMI collaboration machine.
```

I would add one bridge file to each repo:

```text
o---o/IMPLEMENTATION_SURFACES.md
omi-portal/DOCUMENTATION_SURFACES.md
```

With the doctrine:

```text
The documentation repo defines the protocol.
The portal repo instantiates the protocol.
Neither replaces the other.
```

And the strongest repo boundary statement:

```text
o---o asks: What is the OMI protocol?
omi-portal asks: How do people collaborate through it?
```

The important warning is to keep `omi-portal` from becoming only a demo folder. Your `AGENTS.md` already says `demos/` are reference-only snapshots and `dev-docs/` are source/reference material, not wholesale canonical docs. filecite turn24file0 L8-L12 That means the root app surface should stay focused on usable collaboration: inbox, object inspection, import/export, trust/receipt state, and live protocol projection.

One-line repo map:

```text
o---o is the abstract self-enclosed OMI document surface; omi-portal is the public collaboration application surface where RULES, FACTS, CLOSURES, COMBINATORS, CONS, browser UI, kernels, tests, and receipts become executable.
```
